   211	    """Snapshot the full dirty worktree (tracked + untracked) to a backup ref.
   212	
   213	    Captures partial work that ``reset --hard`` would otherwise destroy
   214	    (review F-003 / F-010 safety). ``exclude`` (the run root) is left out so the
   215	    snapshot — and the subsequent reset — never touch the run bookkeeping.
   216	    Returns the backup commit SHA.
   217	    """
   218	    _run(repo, "add", "-A", *_exclude_pathspec(exclude))
   219	    tree = _run(repo, "write-tree").strip()
   220	    parent = head_sha(repo)
   221	    backup = _run(repo, "commit-tree", tree, "-p", parent, "-m", message).strip()
   222	    _run(repo, "update-ref", ref, backup)
   223	    return backup

254	    def _resume_disposition(
   255	        self, step: Step, spec, rec: StepRecord
   256	    ) -> StepResult | None:
   257	        """Decide how to re-enter a step that was interrupted (review F-003).
   258	
   259	        Returns a terminal StepResult to short-circuit (park), or ``None`` to
   260	        proceed with a normal (re-)run. Only repo-writing agent steps that left
   261	        a dirty worktree are parked/reset; idempotent steps (shell) and the
   262	        commit step (which reconciles from git log) simply re-run.
   263	        """
   264	        if rec.base_sha is None or not spec.step_touches_worktree(step):
   265	            return None
   266	        # agent_task killed mid-edit AND adversarial_cycle killed mid-round are
   267	        # both non-idempotent worktree writers: park (or reset) on a dirty base
   268	        # rather than re-running over partial fixer edits / unmanifested
   269	        # fix-round commits. shell and commit re-enter safely on their own.
   270	        is_agent_write = (
   271	            step.type == "agent_task" and spec.step_requires_repo_write(step)
   272	        ) or step.type == "adversarial_cycle"
   273	        if not is_agent_write:
   274	            return None
   275	        # Detect partial work against the narrow bookkeeping exclusion, so a
   276	        # partial *artifact* under the run root (not just a repo-root file) is
   277	        # still seen as a mid-edit interruption (review F-001).
   278	        if not gitops.is_dirty_vs(self.repo_root, rec.base_sha, exclude=self.excludes):
   279	            return None  # clean re-entry: agent never progressed; safe to re-run
   280	        if self.config.interrupted_step == "reset_to_base":
   281	            ts = self.clock().replace(":", "-")
   282	            backup = f"refs/gauntlet/backup/{self.manifest.run_id}/{rec.id}-{ts}"
   283	            # Snapshot the partial work (tracked + untracked) before discarding.
   284	            gitops.backup_dirty_worktree(
   285	                self.repo_root, backup, f"interrupted {rec.id} partial work",
   286	                exclude=self.excludes,
   287	            )
   288	            gitops.reset_hard(self.repo_root, rec.base_sha)
   289	            # `clean` is broader than the dirty check on purpose: it spares the
   290	            # whole run root so the reset never wipes the run pointer, manifests,
   291	            # the authored prd.md, or prior declared artifacts — the re-run
   292	            # regenerates its own outputs over them.
   293	            gitops.clean_untracked(self.repo_root, exclude=[self.config.run_root])
   294	            return None  # tree restored to base; re-run cleanly
   295	        return StepResult(
   296	            status=INTERRUPTED,
   297	            notes=(
   298	                "interrupted mid-edit: worktree dirty vs base SHA "
   299	                f"{rec.base_sha[:10]}; parked for a human (F-003, "
   300	                "interrupted_step=park)"
   301	            ),
   302	        )
   303	
   304	    def _apply_budget_guard(
   305	        self, step: Step, rec: StepRecord, result: StepResult
   306	    ) -> StepResult:
   307	        if result.status != DONE or result.usage is None:
   308	            return result
   309	        budget = step.budget_usd
   310	        if budget is None and step.agent and step.agent in self.config.agents:
   311	            budget = self.config.profile(step.agent).budget_usd
   312	        if budget is None or result.usage.cost_usd is None:
   313	            return result
   314	        projected = (rec.usage.cost_usd or 0.0) + result.usage.cost_usd
   315	        if projected > budget:
   316	            return StepResult(
   317	                status=HALTED,
   318	                usage=result.usage,
   319	                session_id=result.session_id,
   320	                notes=(
   321	                    f"budget halt (FR-3.3): step cost ${projected:.4f} exceeds "
   322	                    f"budget ${budget:.4f}; halting at checkpoint"
   323	                ),
   324	            )
   325	        return result
   326	
   327	    def _finalize(self, rec: StepRecord, result: StepResult) -> None:
   328	        rec.status = {
   329	            DONE: M.DONE,
   330	            FAILED: M.FAILED,
   331	            PARKED: M.PARKED,
   332	            HALTED: M.HALTED,
   333	            SKIPPED: M.SKIPPED,
   334	            INTERRUPTED: M.INTERRUPTED,
   335	        }[result.status]
   336	        rec.ended = self.clock()
   337	        if result.session_id:
   338	            rec.session_id = result.session_id
   339	        if result.usage is not None:
   340	            rec.usage.add(result.usage)
   341	            self.manifest.totals.add(result.usage)
   342	        if result.notes:
   343	            rec.notes = result.notes
   344	        if result.commit_sha:
   345	            self.manifest.commits.append(
   346	                M.CommitRecord(
   347	                    step_id=rec.id,
   348	                    phase=result.commit_phase or "",
   349	                    sha=result.commit_sha,
   350	                )
   351	            )
   352	        for phase, sha in result.commits:  # multi-commit steps (adversarial_cycle)
   353	            self.manifest.commits.append(
   354	                M.CommitRecord(step_id=rec.id, phase=phase, sha=sha)
   355	            )
   356	        if result.status == DONE:
   357	            for name, path in result.artifact_writes.items():
   358	                self.artifacts[name] = path
   359	
   360	    # ---- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------
   361	    def _make_context(
   362	        self, step: Step, rec: StepRecord, iteration: str | None, item: Any
   363	    ) -> StepContext:
   364	        return StepContext(
   365	            repo_root=self.repo_root,
   366	            run_dir=self.run_dir,
   367	            artifact_root=self.artifact_root,
   368	            config=self.config,
   369	            pipeline=self.pipeline,
   370	            manifest=self.manifest,
   371	            record=rec,
   372	            writer=self.writer,
   373	            judge_env=self.judge_env,
   374	            artifacts=dict(self.artifacts),
   375	            excludes=self.excludes,
   376	            iteration_item=item,
   377	            iteration_index=int(iteration) if iteration is not None else None,
   378	            adapter_factory=self.adapter_factory,
   379	        )
   380	
   381	    def _context(self, item: Any = None, iteration: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
   382	        ctx: dict[str, Any] = {
   383	            "config": self.config,
   384	            "artifacts": {name: True for name in self._existing_artifacts()},
   385	            "vars": self.extra_context,
   386	        }
   387	        ctx.update(self.extra_context)
   388	        if item is not None:
   389	            ctx["item"] = item
   390	        return ctx
   391	
   392	    def _seed_artifacts(self) -> None:
   393	        for name in ("prd.md", "plan.md"):
   394	            path = self.artifact_root / name
   395	            if path.exists():
   396	                self.artifacts[name] = path
   397	
   398	    def _existing_artifacts(self) -> set[str]:
   399	        names = set(self.artifacts)
   400	        for name in ("prd.md", "plan.md"):
   401	            if (self.artifact_root / name).exists():
   402	                names.add(name)
   403	        return names
   404	
   405	    def _mark_skipped(self, step_id: str, iteration: str | None) -> None:
   406	        rec = self.manifest.record(step_id, iteration)
   407	        if rec is None:
   408	            rec = StepRecord(id=step_id, type="", iteration=iteration)
   409	            self.manifest.upsert(rec)
   410	        rec.status = M.SKIPPED
   411	        rec.ended = self.clock()
   412	
   413	    def _find_parked_gate(self, step_id: str) -> StepRecord:
   414	        # Scan across iterations so a gate parked inside a foreach (record
   415	        # `gate` with iteration `1`) is reachable by approve/reject (F-004).
   416	        for rec in self.manifest.steps:
   417	            if rec.id == step_id and rec.status == M.PARKED:
   418	                return rec
   419	        existing = self.manifest.record(step_id)
   420	        raise ValueError(
   421	            f"step {step_id!r} is not parked at a gate "
   422	            f"(status: {existing.status if existing else 'absent'})"
   423	        )
   424	
   425	    def _head_sha(self) -> str:
   426	        return gitops.head_sha(self.repo_root)
   427	
   428	    def _set_run_status(self, step_status: str) -> str:
   429	        self.manifest.status = {
   430	            DONE: M.RUN_DONE,
   431	            PARKED: M.RUN_PARKED,
   432	            HALTED: M.RUN_PARKED,  # a halt parks the run for a human (FR-3.3)
   433	            INTERRUPTED: M.RUN_PARKED,  # mid-edit interruption parks (F-003)
   434	            FAILED: M.RUN_FAILED,
   435	        }.get(step_status, M.RUN_RUNNING)
   436	        if self.manifest.status in (M.RUN_DONE,):
   437	            self.manifest.current_step = None
   438	        self._persist()
   439	        return self.manifest.status
   440	
   441	    def _persist(self) -> None:
   442	        self.manifest.write_atomic(self.manifest_path)
   443	        # RUN.md (FR-4.3) is regenerated on every checkpoint so the index never
   444	        # lags the state machine; it is derived data, cheap to rewrite.
   445	        write_run_index(self.run_dir, self.manifest, self.writer)

1	"""Transcript logger (FR-4): the durable, human-readable record of a run.
     2	
     3	Per step (and per ``adversarial_cycle`` sub-step), three files land in the
     4	FR-4.1 layout, all through the :class:`RedactingWriter` (FR-4.4 — redaction
     5	happens before any byte reaches disk, because these logs target git):
     6	
     7	- ``prompt.md``      the exact prompt sent (FR-4.1)
     8	- ``transcript.md``  faithful rendering of every message — prompts, assistant
     9	                     turns, tool calls + results, final output. Nothing
    10	                     summarized away (FR-4.2).
    11	- ``events.jsonl``   the lossless raw event stream (FR-4.2)
    12	- a structured-output file (``findings.json`` etc.) when the step is schema'd
    13	
    14	``RUN.md`` (FR-4.3) is the per-run index: every step's transcript link,
    15	verdict, duration, and cost, regenerated from the manifest so it is always
    16	consistent with the state machine.
    17	
    18	The renderer understands the three adapter event shapes (claude json/
    19	stream-json, codex JSONL, api.completion) and falls back to a fenced JSON dump
    20	for anything else — unknown events are preserved, never dropped. Codex
    21	sandbox refusals surface only as an OS errno inside ``agent_message`` text on
    22	codex-cli 0.139.0 (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #11); the renderer flags those lines so the
    23	refusal is findable in the transcript.
    24	"""
    25	
    26	from __future__ import annotations
    27	
    28	import json
    29	from datetime import datetime
    30	from pathlib import Path
    31	from typing import Any
    32	
    33	from gauntlet.adapters.base import AgentResult
    34	from gauntlet.logging.redact import RedactingWriter
    35	
    36	# FR-4.5: written into the scaffolded .gitignore guidance by `gauntlet init`
    37	# (P6 ships the init command; the text is the P4 logger's contract).
    38	GITIGNORE_GUIDANCE = """\
    39	# Gauntlet run logs (FR-4.5)
    40	#
    41	# Manifests and transcripts are designed to be committed: they are the audit
    42	# trail a reviewer uses to reconstruct every decision. The raw event streams
    43	# (events.jsonl) are lossless but heavy; teams that find them too large for
    44	# git can uncomment the exclusion below — transcript.md remains the faithful
    45	# committable record.
    46	#
    47	# runs/*/run-*/steps/**/events.jsonl
    48	#
    49	# The live run-instance directory ignores itself (the engine writes a
    50	# `.gitignore` containing `*` into it); finalized artifacts you want tracked
    51	# should be copied/committed deliberately, not swept in by `git add -A`.
    52	"""
    53	
    54	_SANDBOX_REFUSAL_MARKER = "operation not permitted"
    55	
    56	
    57	class StepLogger:
    58	    """Writes one step's FR-4.1 file set through the redacting writer."""
    59	
    60	    def __init__(self, writer: RedactingWriter, step_dir: Path) -> None:
    61	        self.writer = writer
    62	        self.step_dir = Path(step_dir)
    63	
    64	    def log_prompt(self, prompt: str) -> None:
    65	        self.writer.write_text(self.step_dir / "prompt.md", prompt)
    66	
    67	    def log_result(
    68	        self, result: AgentResult, *, structured_name: str = "structured.json"
    69	    ) -> None:
    70	        """Persist transcript.md + events.jsonl (+ structured output) for one
    71	        adapter invocation. Lossless: every raw event is written."""
    72	        self.writer.write_text(
    73	            self.step_dir / "transcript.md",
    74	            render_transcript(result.raw_events, final_text=result.text),
    75	        )
    76	        events_path = self.step_dir / "events.jsonl"
    77	        if not result.raw_events:
    78	            # the lossless record exists even when an adapter reported no
    79	            # events (e.g. test fakes) — absence would read as "not captured"
    80	            self.writer.write_text(events_path, "")
    81	        for event in result.raw_events:
    82	            self.writer.append_jsonl(events_path, event)
    83	        if result.structured is not None:
    84	            self.writer.write_text(
    85	                self.step_dir / structured_name,
    86	                json.dumps(result.structured, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),
    87	            )
    88	
    89	    def log_text(self, name: str, text: str) -> None:
    90	        self.writer.write_text(self.step_dir / name, text)
    91	
    92	
    93	# --- transcript rendering (FR-4.2) -------------------------------------------
    94	def render_transcript(events: list[dict[str, Any]], *, final_text: str = "") -> str:
    95	    parts: list[str] = ["# Transcript\n"]
    96	    for event in events:
    97	        parts.append(_render_event(event))
    98	    if final_text:
    99	        parts.append(f"## Final output\n\n{final_text}\n")
   100	    return "\n".join(parts)
   101	
   102	
   103	def _render_event(event: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   104	    etype = event.get("type", "")
   105	    # -- claude shapes ---------------------------------------------------------
   106	    if etype == "system":
   107	        return _kv_block("system", {
   108	            "subtype": event.get("subtype"),
   109	            "session_id": event.get("session_id"),
   110	            "model": event.get("model"),
   111	        })
   112	    if etype == "assistant" or etype == "user":
   113	        return _render_claude_message(etype, event.get("message") or {})
   114	    if etype == "result":
   115	        body = event.get("result") or ""
   116	        meta = _kv_block("result", {
   117	            "subtype": event.get("subtype"),
   118	            "is_error": event.get("is_error"),
   119	            "total_cost_usd": event.get("total_cost_usd"),
   120	        })
   121	        return f"{meta}\n{body}\n"
   122	    # -- codex shapes ----------------------------------------------------------
   123	    if etype == "thread.started":
   124	        return _kv_block("codex thread.started", {"thread_id": event.get("thread_id")})
   125	    if etype == "item.completed":
   126	        return _render_codex_item(event.get("item") or {})
   127	    if etype in ("turn.completed", "turn.failed"):
   128	        return _kv_block(f"codex {etype}", {"usage": event.get("usage"),
   129	                                            "error": event.get("error")})
   130	    # -- api shape ---------------------------------------------------------------
   131	    if etype == "api.completion":
   132	        return _render_api_completion(event.get("response"))
   133	    # -- gauntlet bookkeeping / unknown: preserve verbatim -----------------------
   134	    return f"## event: {etype or 'unknown'}\n\n```json\n{_dump(event)}\n```\n"
   135	
   136	
   137	def _render_claude_message(role: str, message: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   138	    parts = [f"## {role}\n"]
   139	    content = message.get("content")
   140	    if isinstance(content, str):
   141	        parts.append(content + "\n")
   142	        return "\n".join(parts)
   143	    for block in content or []:
   144	        btype = block.get("type")
   145	        if btype == "text":
   146	            parts.append(block.get("text", "") + "\n")
   147	        elif btype == "tool_use":
   148	            parts.append(
   149	                f"**tool call** `{block.get('name')}` (id {block.get('id')})\n\n"
   150	                f"```json\n{_dump(block.get('input'))}\n```\n"
   151	            )
   152	        elif btype == "tool_result":
   153	            body = block.get("content")
   154	            if isinstance(body, list):
   155	                body = "\n".join(
   156	                    b.get("text", _dump(b)) if isinstance(b, dict) else str(b)
   157	                    for b in body
   158	                )
   159	            error = " (is_error)" if block.get("is_error") else ""
   160	            parts.append(
   161	                f"**tool result**{error} (for {block.get('tool_use_id')})\n\n"
   162	                f"```\n{body}\n```\n"
   163	            )
   164	        else:
   165	            parts.append(f"```json\n{_dump(block)}\n```\n")
   166	    return "\n".join(parts)
   167	
   168	
   169	def _render_codex_item(item: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   170	    itype = item.get("type")
   171	    if itype == "agent_message":
   172	        text = item.get("text", "")
   173	        note = ""
   174	        if _SANDBOX_REFUSAL_MARKER in text:
   175	            # codex 0.139.0 emits no command_execution event for a command the
   176	            # sandbox refuses; the errno in agent text is the only signal
   177	            # (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #11). Flag it so the refusal is findable.
   178	            note = (
   179	                "\n> ⚠ contains a sandbox-refusal errno "
   180	                "(no command_execution event is emitted for refused commands "
   181	                "on codex-cli 0.139.0 — BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #11)\n"
   182	            )
   183	        return f"## codex agent_message\n{note}\n{text}\n"
   184	    if itype == "command_execution":
   185	        return (
   186	            f"## codex command_execution\n\n"
   187	            f"```\n$ {item.get('command', '')}\n"
   188	            f"exit: {item.get('exit_code')}\n"
   189	            f"{item.get('aggregated_output', '')}\n```\n"
   190	        )
   191	    if itype == "reasoning":
   192	        return f"## codex reasoning\n\n{item.get('text', '')}\n"
   193	    return f"## codex item: {itype}\n\n```json\n{_dump(item)}\n```\n"
   194	
   195	
   196	def _render_api_completion(response: Any) -> str:
   197	    if isinstance(response, dict):
   198	        try:
   199	            content = response["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
   200	        except (KeyError, IndexError, TypeError):
   201	            content = None
   202	        if content is not None:
   203	            usage = response.get("usage")
   204	            return (
   205	                f"## api completion\n\n{content}\n\n"
   206	                f"_usage: {_dump(usage)}_\n"
   207	            )
   208	    return f"## api completion (raw)\n\n```json\n{_dump(response)}\n```\n"
   209	
   210	
   211	def _kv_block(title: str, fields: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
   212	    lines = [f"## {title}\n"]
   213	    for key, value in fields.items():
   214	        if value is not None:
   215	            lines.append(f"- {key}: `{value}`")
   216	    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
   217	
   218	
   219	def _dump(obj: Any) -> str:
   220	    try:
   221	        return json.dumps(obj, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)
   222	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
   223	        return repr(obj)
   224	
   225	
   226	# --- RUN.md index (FR-4.3) ----------------------------------------------------
   227	def write_run_index(run_dir: Path, manifest: Any, writer: RedactingWriter) -> None:
   228	    """Regenerate RUN.md from the manifest. Idempotent; called on every
   229	    checkpoint so the index never lags the state machine."""
   230	    lines = [
   231	        f"# Run {manifest.run_id} — `{manifest.slug}`\n",
   232	        f"- branch: `{manifest.branch}` (base `{manifest.base_branch}`)",
   233	        f"- pipeline: `{manifest.pipeline.name}` v{manifest.pipeline.version} "
   234	        f"(`{manifest.pipeline.hash[:19]}…`)",
   235	        f"- status: **{manifest.status}**"
   236	        + (f" (at `{manifest.current_step}`)" if manifest.current_step else ""),
   237	        f"- totals: {_fmt_usage(manifest.totals)}",
   238	        "",
   239	        "| step | type | status | duration | usage | notes |",
   240	        "|---|---|---|---|---|---|",
   241	    ]
   242	    for rec in manifest.steps:
   243	        leaf = rec.id if rec.iteration is None else f"{rec.id}.{rec.iteration}"
   244	        step_dir = run_dir / "steps" / leaf
   245	        name = (
   246	            f"[{leaf}](steps/{leaf}/transcript.md)"
   247	            if (step_dir / "transcript.md").exists()
   248	            else f"[{leaf}](steps/{leaf}/)" if step_dir.exists() else leaf
   249	        )
   250	        lines.append(
   251	            f"| {name} | {rec.type} | {rec.status} | {_duration(rec)} "
   252	            f"| {_fmt_usage(rec.usage)} | {_cell(rec.notes)} |"
   253	        )
   254	    if manifest.commits:
   255	        lines += ["", "## Commits", ""]
   256	        lines += [
   257	            f"- `{c.sha[:10]}` {c.phase} (step `{c.step_id}`)"
   258	            for c in manifest.commits
   259	        ]
   260	    writer.write_text(run_dir / "RUN.md", "\n".join(lines) + "\n")
   261	
   262	
   263	def _duration(rec: Any) -> str:
   264	    if not rec.started or not rec.ended:
   265	        return "—"
   266	    try:
   267	        delta = datetime.fromisoformat(rec.ended) - datetime.fromisoformat(rec.started)
   268	        return f"{delta.total_seconds():.0f}s"
   269	    except ValueError:
   270	        return "—"
   271	
   272	
   273	def _fmt_usage(usage: Any) -> str:
   274	    if usage is None:
   275	        return "—"
   276	    tokens = f"{usage.input_tokens or 0}in/{usage.output_tokens or 0}out"
   277	    if usage.cost_usd is not None:
   278	        return f"{tokens} ${usage.cost_usd:.4f}"
   279	    return f"{tokens} (tokens only)"  # degraded path, PRD §12 Q3
   280	
   281	
   282	def _cell(text: str | None) -> str:
   283	    if not text:
   284	        return ""
   285	    return text.replace("|", "\\|").replace("\n", " ")

1	"""Built-in step types: agent_task, shell, human_gate, commit (FR-5, FR-9.2).
     2	
     3	The ``adversarial_cycle`` step type (the review→triage→fix→confirm primitive)
     4	is a P4 deliverable and registers there; P3 ships the four primitives the
     5	crash test and switchover need. Control flow (routing, retries, parking,
     6	budget halts) is the orchestrator's; handlers report status only.
     7	
     8	Trust model (plan §0 / review F-001): ``shell`` commands come **only** from
     9	human-committed pipeline/config YAML — :func:`render_shell_command` refuses any
    10	template token that is not a ``{{config.*}}`` reference, so agent-authored text
    11	can never be substituted into a command line.
    12	"""
    13	
    14	from __future__ import annotations
    15	
    16	import re
    17	import subprocess
    18	from pathlib import Path
    19	
    20	from gauntlet.engine.commit_format import header_prefix, validate_commit_message
    21	from gauntlet.engine.execution import (
    22	    DONE,
    23	    FAILED,
    24	    HALTED,
    25	    PARKED,
    26	    StepContext,
    27	    StepResult,
    28	    StepSpec,
    29	)
    30	from gauntlet.engine import gitops
    31	from gauntlet.engine.pipeline import Step
    32	from gauntlet.logging.transcript import StepLogger
    33	
    34	_CONFIG_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{\s*config\.([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\s*\}\}")
    35	_ANY_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{.*?\}\}")
    36	
    37	
    38	# --- shell -------------------------------------------------------------------
    39	def render_shell_command(template: str, config) -> str:
    40	    """Substitute only ``{{config.<key>}}`` tokens; reject anything else.
    41	
    42	    Refusing non-config tokens is the engine-side enforcement of the trust
    43	    model: no agent-authored artifact may be interpolated into a shell command.
    44	    """
    45	    def _sub(m: re.Match[str]) -> str:
    46	        key = m.group(1)
    47	        value = getattr(config, key, None)
    48	        if value is None:
    49	            raise ValueError(
    50	                f"shell template references unknown config key {key!r}"
    51	            )
    52	        return str(value)
    53	
    54	    rendered = _CONFIG_TOKEN_RE.sub(_sub, template)
    55	    leftover = _ANY_TOKEN_RE.search(rendered)
    56	    if leftover:
    57	        raise ValueError(
    58	            f"shell command may only reference {{{{config.*}}}}; refusing "
    59	            f"to substitute {leftover.group(0)!r} (trust model / review F-001)"
    60	        )
    61	    return rendered
    62	
    63	
    64	def handle_shell(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> StepResult:
    65	    template = step.get("run")
    66	    if not template:
    67	        return StepResult(status=FAILED, notes="shell step has no `run:` command")
    68	    command = render_shell_command(template, ctx.config)
    69	    timeout = step.timeout_s  # per-step guard (FR-3.3); None => unbounded
    70	    try:
    71	        proc = subprocess.run(
    72	            command,
    73	            shell=True,
    74	            cwd=ctx.repo_root,
    75	            capture_output=True,
    76	            text=True,
    77	            timeout=timeout,
    78	        )
    79	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
    80	        _write_step_log(ctx, "output.txt", f"$ {command}\n--- TIMEOUT after {timeout}s ---\n")
    81	        # Halt at a checkpoint rather than letting a stuck command burn on.
    82	        return StepResult(
    83	            status=HALTED,
    84	            notes=f"shell timeout halt (FR-3.3): `{command}` exceeded {timeout}s",
    85	        )
    86	    _write_step_log(ctx, "output.txt", _proc_log(command, proc))
    87	    if proc.returncode != 0:
    88	        return StepResult(
    89	            status=FAILED,
    90	            notes=f"`{command}` exited {proc.returncode}",
    91	        )
    92	    return StepResult(status=DONE, notes=f"`{command}` exited 0")
    93	
    94	
    95	# --- human_gate --------------------------------------------------------------
    96	def handle_human_gate(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> StepResult:
    97	    show = step.get("show", []) or []
    98	    return StepResult(
    99	        status=PARKED,
   100	        notes=f"awaiting human decision; review: {', '.join(show) or '(nothing listed)'}",
   101	    )
   102	
   103	
   104	# --- agent_task --------------------------------------------------------------
   105	def handle_agent_task(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> StepResult:
   106	    agent_name = step.agent
   107	    if not agent_name:
   108	        return StepResult(status=FAILED, notes="agent_task step has no `agent:`")
   109	    adapter = ctx.build_adapter(agent_name)
   110	    prompt = _render_prompt(step, ctx)
   111	    schema = _load_schema(step, ctx)
   112	    # Per-step timeout overrides the profile's step_timeout_s, which overrides
   113	    # the adapter default (FR-3.3). A timeout raises AgentTimeoutError, which the
   114	    # orchestrator turns into a HALTED checkpoint.
   115	    timeout = step.timeout_s
   116	    if timeout is None and agent_name in ctx.config.agents:
   117	        timeout = ctx.config.profile(agent_name).step_timeout_s
   118	    if timeout is not None and hasattr(adapter, "timeout_s"):
   119	        adapter.timeout_s = timeout
   120	    logger = step_logger(ctx)
   121	    logger.log_prompt(prompt)  # before the call: the prompt survives a crash
   122	    result = adapter.run(
   123	        prompt,
   124	        session=ctx.record.session_id,
   125	        schema=schema,
   126	        cwd=ctx.repo_root,
   127	    )
   128	    logger.log_result(result)  # transcript.md + events.jsonl (+ structured)
   129	    artifact_writes: dict[str, Path] = {}
   130	    output = step.get("output")
   131	    if output:
   132	        out_path = ctx.artifact_root / output
   133	        ctx.writer.write_text(out_path, result.text)
   134	        artifact_writes[output] = out_path
   135	    return StepResult(
   136	        status=DONE,
   137	        session_id=result.session_id,
   138	        usage=result.usage,
   139	        artifact_writes=artifact_writes,
   140	        notes=f"agent {agent_name!r} completed",
   141	    )
   142	
   143	
   144	def _render_prompt(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> str:
   145	    template_ref = step.get("prompt")
   146	    if template_ref:
   147	        template_path = ctx.repo_root / template_ref
   148	        base = template_path.read_text()
   149	    else:
   150	        base = step.get("prompt_text", "") or ""
   151	    parts = [base]
   152	    for name in step.get("inputs", []) or []:
   153	        path = ctx.artifacts.get(name) or (ctx.artifact_root / name)
   154	        content = Path(path).read_text() if Path(path).exists() else ""
   155	        parts.append(f"\n\n--- input artifact: {name} ---\n{content}")
   156	    if ctx.iteration_item is not None:
   157	        parts.append(f"\n\n--- foreach item [{ctx.iteration_index}] ---\n{ctx.iteration_item}")
   158	    return "".join(parts)
   159	
   160	
   161	def _load_schema(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> dict | None:
   162	    ref = step.get("findings_schema") or step.get("schema")
   163	    if not ref:
   164	        return None
   165	    import json
   166	
   167	    return json.loads((ctx.repo_root / ref).read_text())

   169	
   170	# --- commit (FR-9.2/9.7) -----------------------------------------------------
   171	def handle_commit(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> StepResult:
   172	    repo = ctx.repo_root
   173	    # Narrow exclusion (review F-001): commit real artifacts (plan.md, outputs);
   174	    # keep only the engine's own bookkeeping out of the commit and the checks.
   175	    exclude = ctx.excludes
   176	    message, draft_usage, draft_session = _commit_message(step, ctx)
   177	    err = validate_commit_message(message)
   178	    if err is not None:
   179	        # message_agent drafting includes a bounded redraft loop in _draft;
   180	        # a literal/exhausted message that still fails is a hard error.
   181	        return StepResult(
   182	            status=FAILED,
   183	            usage=draft_usage,
   184	            session_id=draft_session,
   185	            notes=f"commit message invalid: {err.reason}",
   186	        )
   187	    prefix = header_prefix(message)
   188	
   189	    # Mid-commit resume reconciliation (review F-003): if a prior attempt
   190	    # already created the commit (HEAD moved off the recorded base) but died
   191	    # before recording the SHA, adopt that commit rather than double-committing.
   192	    base = ctx.record.base_sha
   193	    if base and gitops.head_sha(repo) != base and gitops.is_clean(repo, exclude=exclude):
   194	        existing = gitops.head_sha(repo)
   195	        if header_prefix(gitops.commit_message(repo, existing)) == prefix:
   196	            return StepResult(
   197	                status=DONE,
   198	                commit_sha=existing,
   199	                commit_phase=prefix,
   200	                usage=draft_usage,
   201	                session_id=draft_session,
   202	                notes="reconciled pre-existing commit after mid-commit interruption",
   203	            )
   204	
   205	    if gitops.is_clean(repo, exclude=exclude):
   206	        return StepResult(
   207	            status=FAILED,
   208	            usage=draft_usage,
   209	            session_id=draft_session,
   210	            notes="commit step found a clean worktree with nothing to commit",
   211	        )
   212	
   213	    agent_name = step.agent or step.get("message_agent") or "builder"
   214	    identity = ctx.config.identity(agent_name)
   215	    sha = gitops.commit_all(repo, message, identity=identity, exclude=exclude)
   216	    return StepResult(
   217	        status=DONE, commit_sha=sha, commit_phase=prefix,
   218	        usage=draft_usage, session_id=draft_session, notes=f"committed {sha[:10]}",
   219	    )
   220	
   221	
   222	def _commit_message(step: Step, ctx: StepContext):
   223	    """Return ``(message, usage, session_id)``; usage/session are None for a
   224	    literal message (no model call)."""
   225	    literal = step.get("message")
   226	    if literal:
   227	        return literal, None, None  # human-authored YAML; still format-validated
   228	    return _draft_commit_message(step, ctx)
   229	
   230	
   231	def _draft_commit_message(step: Step, ctx: StepContext):
   232	    """Draft a commit message via the message_agent with bounded redraft.
   233	
   234	    The agent sees the change as data — both the tracked diff AND the untracked
   235	    files `git add -A` will sweep in (review F-008: a new-file phase otherwise
   236	    drafts from an empty diff) — plus an optional plan section. The engine
   237	    validates the format and asks for a redraft on violation (FR-9.2). Returns
   238	    ``(message, usage, session_id)`` so the commit step records the drafter's
   239	    cost (FR-3.2/§7).
   240	    """
   241	    agent_name = step.get("message_agent")
   242	    if not agent_name:
   243	        raise ValueError("commit step needs either `message:` or `message_agent:`")
   244	    adapter = ctx.build_adapter(agent_name)
   245	    change = _change_context(ctx)
   246	    base_prompt = (
   247	        (ctx.repo_root / step.get("prompt")).read_text()
   248	        if step.get("prompt")
   249	        else _DEFAULT_COMMIT_PROMPT
   250	    )
   251	    phase_hint = step.get("phase", "")
   252	    plan_section = _plan_section(step, ctx)
   253	    header = (
   254	        f"{base_prompt}\n\nRequired header phase prefix: {phase_hint or '(infer PN)'}\n"
   255	        f"{plan_section}"
   256	    )
   257	    prompt = f"{header}\n{change}\n"
   258	    max_redrafts = int(step.get("max_redrafts", 2))
   259	    message = ""
   260	    usage = _UsageAccumulator()  # sum across ALL draft attempts, incl. rejected
   261	    session_id = None
   262	    for _attempt in range(1 + max_redrafts):
   263	        result = adapter.run(prompt, cwd=ctx.repo_root)
   264	        usage.add(result.usage)  # a redraft's cost is real spend (F-008 round 2)
   265	        session_id = result.session_id
   266	        message = result.text.strip()
   267	        if validate_commit_message(message) is None:
   268	            return message, usage.result(), session_id
   269	        prompt = (
   270	            f"{header}\n\nYour previous draft was rejected: "
   271	            f"{validate_commit_message(message).reason}. "
   272	            f"Return only the corrected commit message.\n{change}\n"
   273	        )
   274	    return message, usage.result(), session_id
   275	
   276	
   277	class _UsageAccumulator:
   278	    """Sum Usage across redraft attempts so rejected drafts still count (F-008)."""
   279	
   280	    def __init__(self) -> None:
   281	        self._in = 0
   282	        self._out = 0
   283	        self._cached = 0
   284	        self._cost: float | None = None
   285	        self._seen = False
   286	
   287	    def add(self, usage) -> None:
   288	        if usage is None:
   289	            return
   290	        self._seen = True
   291	        self._in += usage.input_tokens or 0
   292	        self._out += usage.output_tokens or 0
   293	        self._cached += usage.cached_input_tokens or 0
   294	        if usage.cost_usd is not None:
   295	            self._cost = (self._cost or 0.0) + usage.cost_usd
   296	
   297	    def result(self):
   298	        from gauntlet.adapters.base import Usage
   299	
   300	        if not self._seen:
   301	            return None
   302	        return Usage(
   303	            input_tokens=self._in,
   304	            output_tokens=self._out,
   305	            cached_input_tokens=self._cached,
   306	            cost_usd=self._cost,
   307	        )
   308	
   309	
   310	def _change_context(ctx: StepContext) -> str:
   311	    """The diff vs HEAD plus the untracked files staging will add (F-008)."""
   312	    repo = ctx.repo_root
   313	    diff = gitops.diff_head(repo, exclude=ctx.excludes)
   314	    status = gitops.status_porcelain(repo, exclude=ctx.excludes)
   315	    return (
   316	        f"--- git status (incl. untracked) ---\n{status}\n"
   317	        f"\n--- diff (tracked, vs HEAD) ---\n{diff}"
   318	    )
   319	
   320	
   321	def _plan_section(step: Step, ctx: StepContext) -> str:
   322	    """Optional plan excerpt the message_agent drafts from (FR-9.2)."""
   323	    ref = step.get("plan_section")
   324	    if not ref:
   325	        return ""
   326	    path = ctx.artifacts.get(ref) or (ctx.artifact_root / ref)
   327	    if Path(path).exists():
   328	        return f"\n--- plan section: {ref} ---\n{Path(path).read_text()}\n"
   329	    return ""
   330	
   331	
   332	_DEFAULT_COMMIT_PROMPT = (
   333	    "Draft a git commit message for the staged changes. Line 1: an imperative "
   334	    "header prefixed with the phase, e.g. 'P3: <summary>', at most 72 chars. "
   335	    "Then a blank line, then a body explaining what changed and why, the plan "
   336	    "assumption validated, and relevant FR references."
   337	)

   340	# --- helpers -----------------------------------------------------------------
   341	def _proc_log(command: str, proc: subprocess.CompletedProcess) -> str:
   342	    return (
   343	        f"$ {command}\n--- exit {proc.returncode} ---\n"
   344	        f"--- stdout ---\n{proc.stdout}\n--- stderr ---\n{proc.stderr}\n"
   345	    )

   348	def step_log_dir(ctx: StepContext) -> Path:
   349	    iteration = ctx.record.iteration
   350	    leaf = ctx.record.id if iteration is None else f"{ctx.record.id}.{iteration}"
   351	    return ctx.steps_dir() / leaf

   354	def step_logger(ctx: StepContext, *subdir: str) -> StepLogger:
   355	    """FR-4 logger for this step (or a sub-step, e.g. a cycle round's review)."""
   356	    return StepLogger(ctx.writer, step_log_dir(ctx).joinpath(*subdir))

   359	def _write_step_log(ctx: StepContext, name: str, text: str) -> None:
   360	    ctx.writer.write_text(step_log_dir(ctx) / name, text)

1	"""Redacting writer (FR-4.4; plan P1 review F-005, full list P4).
     2	
     3	No log line the bootstrap writes lands on disk unredacted: transcripts,
     4	captured event streams, and the judge audit log all pass through
     5	:class:`RedactingWriter`. From P4 the transcript logger is its home and the
     6	list is configurable (``redaction:`` in `.gauntlet/config.yaml`), default-on —
     7	these logs are intended for git.
     8	
     9	Design per BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #7:
    10	- Exact matching of known secret env-var *values* is the primary mechanism.
    11	- Credential-pattern regexes are the fallback, and they are boundary-aware
    12	  with minimum lengths (a naive ``sk-`` once matched "ask-with-warning").
    13	- Every hit records *which pattern fired*, so false positives are diagnosable.
    14	"""
    15	
    16	from __future__ import annotations
    17	
    18	import json
    19	import re
    20	from collections.abc import Mapping
    21	from dataclasses import dataclass
    22	from pathlib import Path
    23	from typing import Any
    24	
    25	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
    26	
    27	# Env vars whose names suggest credentials; their *values* get masked.
    28	SECRET_ENV_NAME_RE = re.compile(
    29	    r"(KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASSWORD|PASSWD|CREDENTIAL|AUTH)", re.IGNORECASE
    30	)
    31	
    32	# Values shorter than this are too likely to collide with ordinary text
    33	# (e.g. PASSWORD=dev) to be safely masked by exact match.
    34	MIN_SECRET_VALUE_LENGTH = 8
    35	
    36	# Fallback credential patterns: boundary-aware, with minimum lengths.
    37	# Order matters — more specific prefixes first so the hit names stay precise.
    38	CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS: tuple[tuple[str, re.Pattern[str]], ...] = (
    39	    ("anthropic-key", re.compile(r"\bsk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}\b")),
    40	    ("openai-style-key", re.compile(r"\bsk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}\b")),
    41	    ("github-token", re.compile(r"\b(?:ghp|gho|ghu|ghs|ghr)_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,}\b")),
    42	    ("github-pat", re.compile(r"\bgithub_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{22,}\b")),
    43	    ("gitlab-token", re.compile(r"\bglpat-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}\b")),
    44	    ("aws-access-key-id", re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b")),
    45	    ("slack-token", re.compile(r"\bxox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}\b")),
    46	    ("bearer-token", re.compile(r"\b[Bb]earer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._~+/=-]{20,}")),
    47	    (
    48	        "private-key-block",
    49	        re.compile(
    50	            r"-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----.*?-----END [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----",
    51	            re.DOTALL,
    52	        ),
    53	    ),
    54	)
    55	
    56	
    57	class ExtraPattern(BaseModel):
    58	    """One configured fallback regex (FR-4.4). Compiled at config load so a bad
    59	    pattern fails the run up front, not mid-write."""
    60	
    61	    name: str
    62	    regex: str
    63	
    64	    def compiled(self) -> re.Pattern[str]:
    65	        return re.compile(self.regex, re.DOTALL)
    66	
    67	
    68	class RedactionSettings(BaseModel):
    69	    """The configurable redaction list (FR-4.4), default-on.
    70	
    71	    ``extra_env_vars`` are env-var *names* whose values get masked even when the
    72	    name heuristic (KEY/TOKEN/SECRET/...) would miss them. ``extra_patterns``
    73	    extend the built-in credential regexes — they never replace them; the
    74	    defaults are the floor, configuration only adds.
    75	    """
    76	
    77	    extra_env_vars: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    78	    extra_patterns: list[ExtraPattern] = Field(default_factory=list)
    79	    min_value_length: int = MIN_SECRET_VALUE_LENGTH
    80	
    81	
    82	def build_redactor(
    83	    settings: RedactionSettings | None = None,
    84	    env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
    85	) -> Redactor:
    86	    """Construct a :class:`Redactor` from configured settings (FR-4.4)."""
    87	    settings = settings or RedactionSettings()
    88	    return Redactor(
    89	        env,
    90	        extra_patterns=tuple(
    91	            (p.name, p.compiled()) for p in settings.extra_patterns
    92	        ),
    93	        min_value_length=settings.min_value_length,
    94	        extra_env_names=frozenset(settings.extra_env_vars),
    95	    )
    96	
    97	
    98	def _secret_variants(value: str) -> set[str]:
    99	    """A secret's raw form plus its JSON-escaped serializations."""
   100	    return {
   101	        value,
   102	        json.dumps(value)[1:-1],  # ensure_ascii=True spelling
   103	        json.dumps(value, ensure_ascii=False)[1:-1],
   104	    }
   105	
   106	
   107	@dataclass(frozen=True)
   108	class RedactionHit:
   109	    """One redaction occurrence: which pattern fired, how many times."""
   110	
   111	    pattern: str  # e.g. "env:OPENAI_API_KEY" or "openai-style-key"
   112	    count: int
   113	
   114	
   115	class Redactor:
   116	    """Masks secrets in text. Env-var values first, then pattern fallbacks."""
   117	
   118	    def __init__(
   119	        self,
   120	        env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
   121	        *,
   122	        extra_patterns: tuple[tuple[str, re.Pattern[str]], ...] = (),
   123	        min_value_length: int = MIN_SECRET_VALUE_LENGTH,
   124	        extra_env_names: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
   125	    ) -> None:
   126	        if env is None:
   127	            import os
   128	
   129	            env = os.environ
   130	        # Each secret is matched in its raw form AND its JSON-escaped forms:
   131	        # writers serialize before redacting, and json.dumps escapes quotes,
   132	        # backslashes and newlines, so the raw value alone would miss them
   133	        # (review P1 F-004). Longest variants first so overlaps mask fully.
   134	        self._env_secrets: list[tuple[str, str]] = sorted(
   135	            (
   136	                (name, variant)
   137	                for name, value in env.items()
   138	                if (SECRET_ENV_NAME_RE.search(name) or name in extra_env_names)
   139	                and len(value) >= min_value_length
   140	                for variant in _secret_variants(value)
   141	            ),
   142	            key=lambda item: len(item[1]),
   143	            reverse=True,
   144	        )
   145	        self._patterns = CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS + extra_patterns
   146	
   147	    def redact(self, text: str) -> tuple[str, list[RedactionHit]]:
   148	        """Return ``(redacted_text, hits)``; hits name the patterns that fired."""
   149	        hits: list[RedactionHit] = []
   150	        env_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
   151	        for name, variant in self._env_secrets:
   152	            count = text.count(variant)
   153	            if count:
   154	                text = text.replace(variant, f"[REDACTED:env:{name}]")
   155	                env_counts[name] = env_counts.get(name, 0) + count
   156	        hits.extend(
   157	            RedactionHit(pattern=f"env:{name}", count=count)
   158	            for name, count in env_counts.items()
   159	        )
   160	        for pattern_name, pattern in self._patterns:
   161	            text, count = pattern.subn(f"[REDACTED:{pattern_name}]", text)
   162	            if count:
   163	                hits.append(RedactionHit(pattern=pattern_name, count=count))
   164	        return text, hits
   165	
   166	
   167	class RedactingWriter:
   168	    """File writer that redacts everything it puts on disk.
   169	
   170	    Accumulates hits in ``hits_log`` (path, pattern, count) so a caller can
   171	    audit what fired — including diagnosing false positives.
   172	    """
   173	
   174	    def __init__(self, redactor: Redactor | None = None) -> None:
   175	        self.redactor = redactor or Redactor()
   176	        self.hits_log: list[tuple[Path, RedactionHit]] = []
   177	
   178	    def write_text(self, path: Path | str, text: str) -> list[RedactionHit]:
   179	        path = Path(path)
   180	        redacted, hits = self._record(path, text)
   181	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   182	        path.write_text(redacted)
   183	        return hits
   184	
   185	    def append_line(self, path: Path | str, line: str) -> list[RedactionHit]:
   186	        path = Path(path)
   187	        redacted, hits = self._record(path, line.rstrip("\n"))
   188	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   189	        with path.open("a") as fh:
   190	            fh.write(redacted + "\n")
   191	        return hits
   192	
   193	    def append_jsonl(self, path: Path | str, obj: Any) -> list[RedactionHit]:
   194	        return self.append_line(path, json.dumps(obj, ensure_ascii=False))
   195	
   196	    def _record(self, path: Path, text: str) -> tuple[str, list[RedactionHit]]:
   197	        redacted, hits = self.redactor.redact(text)
   198	        self.hits_log.extend((path, hit) for hit in hits)
   199	        return redacted, hits
