360	gauntlet status [<slug>]
   361	gauntlet approve <slug> [--gate ID] / reject --notes "..."
   362	gauntlet resume <slug> / abort <slug>
   363	gauntlet report <run> [--trend]    # cost + metrics
   364	gauntlet feedback <run>            # capture/append human feedback
   365	gauntlet proposals review          # approve/reject improvement diffs
   366	gauntlet rollback <slug> --phase N
   367	```
   368	
   369	---
   370	
   371	## 7. Data & Schemas (normative excerpts)
   372	
   373	**findings.json** (reviewer output, enforced via `--output-schema` on Codex; schema-prompt + validation/retry on other adapters):
   374	
   375	```json
   376	{ "findings": [ {
   377	    "id": "F-001",
   378	    "severity": "blocking | major | minor | nit",
   379	    "category": "correctness | spec-gap | security | performance | principle-violation | style",
   380	    "location": "file/section reference",
   381	    "claim": "what is wrong",
   382	    "evidence": "why the reviewer believes it",
   383	    "suggested_fix": "optional"
   384	} ] }
   385	```
   386	
   387	**triage.json** (triager output, one verdict per finding — the point-by-point evaluation):
   388	
   389	```json
   390	{ "verdicts": [ {
   391	    "finding_id": "F-001",
   392	    "verdict": "legitimate | bikeshedding | premature_optimization | not_applicable",
   393	    "reasoning": "1-3 sentences",
   394	    "action": "fix_now | defer | reject"
   395	} ] }
   396	```
   397	
   398	**manifest.json**: `{pipeline: {name, version, hash}, prompt_hashes, status, current_step, steps: [{id, status, started, ended, agent, session_id, usage}], commits: [{phase, sha}], totals: {tokens, cost}}`.
   399	
   400	---
   401	
   402	## 8. Security & Privacy
   403	
   404	- Judge service binds to 127.0.0.1 only; per-run shared token in hook env to reject foreign callers.
   405	- Fail-closed posture throughout (FR-7.2); hook-disabling flags are lint-checked out of agent profiles at config load.
   406	- Transcript redaction (FR-4.4) before any write, since logs target source control.
   407	- Codex runs sandboxed `workspace-write`; network grants are explicit per-profile config, default off.
   408	- Prompt-injection containment: reviewer findings are *data* to the triager (wrapped, never executed); judge decisions never derive from agent-authored text alone.
   409	
   410	---
   411	
   412	## 9. Implementation Plan (phased, assumption-validating)
   413	
   414	Each phase ends with passing tests and a commit. Phases are ordered to kill the riskiest assumptions first.

1	"""Run configuration: agent profiles, identities, policy (`.gauntlet/config.yaml`).
     2	
     3	FR-2.1: every step's ``agent:`` references a named profile here, binding an
     4	adapter + model + flags. FR-2.2: swapping builder/reviewer is a YAML edit, no
     5	code change. The engine builds the actual adapter instance from a profile via
     6	the entry-point registry (FR-2.4); the banned-flag lint (PRD §8) runs as a side
     7	effect of constructing the CLI adapters and is invoked explicitly for ``api``.
     8	"""
     9	
    10	from __future__ import annotations
    11	
    12	import inspect
    13	from pathlib import Path
    14	from typing import Any
    15	
    16	import yaml
    17	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
    18	
    19	from gauntlet.adapters import get_adapter_class
    20	from gauntlet.config import lint_flags
    21	from gauntlet.engine.gitops import Identity
    22	from gauntlet.logging.redact import RedactionSettings
    23	
    24	DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH = Path(".gauntlet/config.yaml")
    25	
    26	
    27	class AgentProfile(BaseModel):
    28	    """One named agent profile (FR-2.1). Adapter-specific fields are passed
    29	    through to the adapter constructor; engine-level budget guards (FR-3.3) are
    30	    stripped out before construction."""
    31	
    32	    # extra fields are allowed so a plugin adapter can declare its own flags
    33	    # without a code change here (FR-2.4); they are filtered to the adapter's
    34	    # constructor signature at build time.
    35	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
    36	
    37	    adapter: str
    38	    model: str | None = None
    39	
    40	    # --- engine-level guards (FR-3.3); not passed to the adapter ---
    41	    max_turns: int | None = None
    42	    budget_usd: float | None = None
    43	    step_timeout_s: float | None = None
    44	
    45	    def adapter_class(self) -> type:
    46	        return get_adapter_class(self.adapter)
    47	
    48	    def _adapter_kwargs(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
    49	        guard_fields = {"max_turns", "budget_usd", "step_timeout_s"}
    50	        data = self.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
    51	        data.pop("adapter", None)
    52	        for f in guard_fields:
    53	            data.pop(f, None)
    54	        return data
    55	
    56	    def build_adapter(self) -> Any:
    57	        """Construct the adapter, filtering kwargs to its constructor signature.
    58	
    59	        Unknown profile keys (e.g. an adapter-specific flag the engine has never
    60	        heard of) are dropped rather than crashing, keeping FR-2.4 plugins
    61	        first-class. Banned flags are still rejected: the CLI adapters lint in
    62	        ``__init__``; ``base_flags`` is linted here regardless of adapter.
    63	        """
    64	        cls = self.adapter_class()
    65	        kwargs = self._adapter_kwargs()
    66	        base_flags = kwargs.get("base_flags")
    67	        if isinstance(base_flags, list):
    68	            lint_flags(base_flags)
    69	        sig = inspect.signature(cls.__init__)
    70	        accepted = {
    71	            name
    72	            for name in sig.parameters
    73	            if name not in ("self",)
    74	        }
    75	        # If the constructor takes **kwargs we keep everything; otherwise filter.
    76	        takes_var_kw = any(
    77	            p.kind is inspect.Parameter.VAR_KEYWORD
    78	            for p in sig.parameters.values()
    79	        )
    80	        if takes_var_kw:
    81	            return cls(**kwargs)
    82	        return cls(**{k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if k in accepted})
    83	
    84	    def capabilities(self) -> Any:
    85	        """Declared adapter capabilities (FR-2.3 load-time validation)."""
    86	        return self.adapter_class().capabilities
    87	
    88	
    89	class RunConfig(BaseModel):
    90	    """Top-level `.gauntlet/config.yaml` (FR-2.1, FR-9.1/9.7, F-003 policy)."""
    91	
    92	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
    93	
    94	    base_branch: str = "main"
    95	    branch_prefix: str = "gauntlet/"
    96	    # Single run-root for every artifact of a run (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #2): plan,
    97	    # transcripts, and manifests live under run_root/<slug>/. FR-4.1's
    98	    # ".gauntlet/runs" is this same setting; the bootstrap pins it to "runs".
    99	    run_root: str = "runs"
   100	    test_command: str = "uv run pytest"
   101	    agents: dict[str, AgentProfile] = Field(default_factory=dict)
   102	    identities: dict[str, Identity] = Field(default_factory=dict)
   103	
   104	    # Transaction-boundary policy on resume of a dirty interrupted step (F-003).
   105	    interrupted_step: str = "park"  # park | reset_to_base
   106	
   107	    # Reviewer-mutation policy (FR-9.6): commit | revert | halt.
   108	    reviewer_mutation: str = "commit"
   109	
   110	    # Configurable redaction list (FR-4.4), default-on; the transcript logger
   111	    # builds its Redactor from this.
   112	    redaction: RedactionSettings = Field(default_factory=RedactionSettings)
   113	
   114	    def profile(self, name: str) -> AgentProfile:
   115	        try:
   116	            return self.agents[name]
   117	        except KeyError:
   118	            raise KeyError(
   119	                f"no agent profile named {name!r}; known: {sorted(self.agents)}"
   120	            ) from None
   121	
   122	    def identity(self, agent_name: str) -> Identity:
   123	        """Commit identity for an agent (FR-9.7); falls back to a generic one."""
   124	        if agent_name in self.identities:
   125	            return self.identities[agent_name]
   126	        return Identity(
   127	            name=f"Gauntlet {agent_name}",
   128	            email=f"{agent_name}@gauntlet.local",
   129	        )
   130	
   131	    @classmethod
   132	    def load(cls, path: Path = DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH) -> RunConfig:
   133	        if not path.exists():
   134	            raise FileNotFoundError(
   135	                f"run config not found at {path}; `gauntlet init` scaffolds it (P6)"
   136	            )
   137	        data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text()) or {}
   138	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
   139	            raise ValueError(f"{path} must be a YAML mapping, got {type(data).__name__}")
   140	        return cls.model_validate(data)

1	"""Commit-message format validation (FR-9.2/9.4, CLAUDE.md §7).
     2	
     3	The ``commit`` step has the ``message_agent`` draft a message; the engine then
     4	validates it against the enforced format and rejects + redrafts on violation.
     5	The message is *data* (model-authored), so it is validated, never trusted.
     6	
     7	Accepted header forms (the phase prefix is structural, not free text):
     8	- ``P3: <imperative summary>``            phase commit (FR-9.2)
     9	- ``P3.1: Address review — <summary>``    fix round (FR-9.4)
    10	- ``P3.r1: Reviewer-applied changes — …`` reviewer-attributed (FR-9.6, P4)
    11	"""
    12	
    13	from __future__ import annotations
    14	
    15	import re
    16	from dataclasses import dataclass
    17	
    18	HEADER_MAX = 72
    19	
    20	# P<n>, P<n>.<round>, or P<n>.r<round>, then ": " then a non-empty summary.
    21	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^P\d+(?:\.\d+|\.r\d+)?: \S.*$")
    22	
    23	
    24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    25	class FormatError:
    26	    reason: str
    27	
    28	
    29	def validate_commit_message(message: str) -> FormatError | None:
    30	    """Return ``None`` if the message is well-formed, else a :class:`FormatError`.
    31	
    32	    Rules (single source of truth for the engine's reject + redraft loop):
    33	    1. A header line matching the ``PN[.x|.rx]: summary`` shape, ≤ 72 chars.
    34	    2. A blank second line.
    35	    3. A non-empty body (the reasoning — what/why, refs, deferrals).
    36	    """
    37	    if not message or not message.strip():
    38	        return FormatError("commit message is empty")
    39	    # Normalize trailing newline handling; keep interior structure intact.
    40	    lines = message.rstrip("\n").split("\n")
    41	    header = lines[0]
    42	    if len(header) > HEADER_MAX:
    43	        return FormatError(
    44	            f"header is {len(header)} chars; must be ≤ {HEADER_MAX} (FR-9.2)"
    45	        )
    46	    if not _HEADER_RE.match(header):
    47	        return FormatError(
    48	            "header must match 'PN: summary' / 'PN.x: ...' / 'PN.rx: ...' "
    49	            f"(got {header!r})"
    50	        )
    51	    if len(lines) < 2 or lines[1].strip() != "":
    52	        return FormatError("second line must be blank (header/body separator)")
    53	    body = "\n".join(lines[2:]).strip()
    54	    if not body:
    55	        return FormatError("body is empty; the reasoning is part of the deliverable")
    56	    return None
    57	
    58	
    59	def header_prefix(message: str) -> str | None:
    60	    """Return the ``PN[.x]`` prefix of a well-formed header, else ``None``.
    61	
    62	    Used by the mid-commit resume reconciliation (review F-003): match a commit
    63	    that already exists in ``git log`` against the phase the engine intended.
    64	    """
    65	    header = message.rstrip("\n").split("\n", 1)[0]
    66	    m = re.match(r"^(P\d+(?:\.\d+|\.r\d+)?):", header)
    67	    return m.group(1) if m else None

1	"""Load-time pipeline validation (FR-5.3, FR-2.3, PRD §8).
     2	
     3	Run once when a pipeline is loaded for a run. Three classes of check:
     4	
     5	* **Dangling artifact dataflow** (FR-5.3): every ``inputs:``/``artifact:``
     6	  reference must be a seed artifact (``prd.md``/``plan.md``) or produced by an
     7	  earlier step's ``output:``.
     8	* **Adapter capabilities** (FR-2.3): a step that writes the repo cannot bind an
     9	  adapter that can't (e.g. ``api``); a step that needs a schema *warns* if its
    10	  adapter only does best-effort JSON, and *errors* if it does none.
    11	* **Banned flags** (§8): constructing each referenced adapter runs the
    12	  permission-bypass / hook-disabling lint; a violation aborts the load.
    13	"""
    14	
    15	from __future__ import annotations
    16	
    17	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    18	
    19	from gauntlet.config import BannedFlagError
    20	from gauntlet.engine.config import RunConfig
    21	from gauntlet.engine.execution import get_spec
    22	from gauntlet.engine.pipeline import Pipeline, Step
    23	
    24	SEED_ARTIFACTS = frozenset({"prd.md", "plan.md"})
    25	
    26	
    27	class PipelineValidationError(ValueError):
    28	    def __init__(self, errors: list[str]) -> None:
    29	        super().__init__("pipeline failed validation:\n- " + "\n- ".join(errors))
    30	        self.errors = errors
    31	
    32	
    33	@dataclass
    34	class ValidationReport:
    35	    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    36	    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    37	
    38	    def ok(self) -> bool:
    39	        return not self.errors
    40	
    41	
    42	def validate_pipeline(
    43	    pipeline: Pipeline, config: RunConfig, *, seeds: frozenset[str] = SEED_ARTIFACTS
    44	) -> ValidationReport:
    45	    report = ValidationReport()
    46	    available: set[str] = set(seeds)
    47	    for stage in pipeline.stages:
    48	        stage_ids = {step.id for step in stage.steps}
    49	        for step in stage.steps:
    50	            _validate_step(step, config, available, report)
    51	            # on_fail routing is stage-local: the runtime jumps within the
    52	            # current stage's step list, so a cross-stage target would validate
    53	            # then crash at runtime (review F-005). Reject it at load.
    54	            if step.on_fail and step.on_fail.route_to not in stage_ids:
    55	                report.errors.append(
    56	                    f"step {step.id!r} on_fail routes to {step.on_fail.route_to!r}, "
    57	                    "which is not a step in the same stage (cross-stage routing "
    58	                    "is unsupported, FR-5.4 / review F-005)"
    59	                )
    60	            output = step.get("output")
    61	            if output:
    62	                available.add(output)
    63	            if step.type == "adversarial_cycle":
    64	                # the cycle registers its round outputs as named artifacts
    65	                available.update({"findings.json", "triage.json", "confirm.json"})
    66	    if report.errors:
    67	        raise PipelineValidationError(report.errors)
    68	    return report
    69	
    70	
    71	def _validate_step(
    72	    step: Step, config: RunConfig, available: set[str], report: ValidationReport
    73	) -> None:
    74	    # 1. step type resolves
    75	    try:
    76	        spec = get_spec(step.type)
    77	    except KeyError as exc:
    78	        report.errors.append(str(exc))
    79	        return
    80	
    81	    # 1b. `max_turns` is unenforceable on the pinned CLIs (claude 2.1.172 has no
    82	    # --max-turns; codex exec has no turn cap) — reject it rather than silently
    83	    # ignore a claimed guard (review F-006). timeout_s + budget_usd are the
    84	    # working per-step halts (FR-3.3).
    85	    if step.max_turns is not None:
    86	        report.errors.append(
    87	            f"step {step.id!r} sets max_turns, but no installed adapter can honor "
    88	            "it; use timeout_s / budget_usd (FR-3.3 / review F-006)"
    89	        )
    90	
    91	    # 2. dangling artifact dataflow (FR-5.3)
    92	    for name in (step.get("inputs", []) or []):
    93	        if name not in available:
    94	            report.errors.append(
    95	                f"step {step.id!r} input {name!r} is not a seed artifact and is "
    96	                "produced by no earlier step (dangling reference, FR-5.3)"
    97	            )
    98	    artifact = step.get("artifact")
    99	    if artifact and artifact not in available:
   100	        report.errors.append(
   101	            f"step {step.id!r} artifact {artifact!r} is not available at this "
   102	            "point (dangling reference, FR-5.3)"
   103	        )
   104	
   105	    # 2b. adversarial_cycle role bindings (FR-5.2): the three core roles are
   106	    # required, and capability checks are role-aware — the FIXER writes the
   107	    # repo (FR-2.3); the REVIEWER is intended read-only (FR-9.6), so a
   108	    # repo-write check on it would be exactly backwards.
   109	    if step.type == "adversarial_cycle":
   110	        for role in ("reviewer", "triager", "fixer"):
   111	            if not step.get(role):
   112	                report.errors.append(
   113	                    f"step {step.id!r} (adversarial_cycle) is missing required "
   114	                    f"role {role!r} (FR-5.2)"
   115	                )
   116	        fixer = step.get("fixer")
   117	        if fixer and fixer in config.agents:
   118	            if not config.profile(fixer).capabilities().repo_write:
   119	                report.errors.append(
   120	                    f"step {step.id!r} fixer {fixer!r} cannot write the repo "
   121	                    "(FR-2.3): fix rounds edit files"
   122	                )
   123	        if step.get("commit_each_fix_round") is False:
   124	            report.errors.append(
   125	                f"step {step.id!r} sets commit_each_fix_round=false, which "
   126	                "breaks the clean-handoff invariant (FR-9.3/9.4); unsupported"
   127	            )
   128	
   129	    # 3. agent profile resolution + capabilities (FR-2.3) + banned flags (§8)
   130	    agent_refs = _agent_refs(step, spec.needs_agent)
   131	    for ref in agent_refs:
   132	        if ref not in config.agents:
   133	            report.errors.append(
   134	                f"step {step.id!r} references undefined agent profile {ref!r}"
   135	            )
   136	            continue
   137	        profile = config.profile(ref)
   138	        if profile.max_turns is not None:
   139	            report.errors.append(
   140	                f"agent profile {ref!r} sets max_turns, unenforceable on the "
   141	                "pinned CLIs; use step_timeout_s / budget_usd (review F-006)"
   142	            )
   143	        caps = profile.capabilities()
   144	        if spec.step_requires_repo_write(step) and not caps.repo_write:
   145	            report.errors.append(
   146	                f"step {step.id!r} needs repo-write but agent {ref!r} "
   147	                f"(adapter {profile.adapter!r}) cannot write the repo (FR-2.3)"
   148	            )
   149	        if _step_uses_schema(step, spec):
   150	            if caps.structured_output == "none":
   151	                report.errors.append(
   152	                    f"step {step.id!r} needs structured output but agent {ref!r} "
   153	                    "supports none (FR-2.3)"
   154	                )
   155	            elif caps.structured_output == "best_effort":
   156	                report.warnings.append(
   157	                    f"step {step.id!r} relies on schema output but agent {ref!r} "
   158	                    "is best-effort JSON only; validate-and-retry applies (FR-2.3)"
   159	                )
   160	        try:
   161	            profile.build_adapter()
   162	        except BannedFlagError as exc:
   163	            report.errors.append(
   164	                f"step {step.id!r} agent {ref!r} uses a banned flag: {exc}"
   165	            )
   166	        except Exception as exc:  # construction issues surface as warnings
   167	            report.warnings.append(
   168	                f"step {step.id!r} agent {ref!r} could not be pre-constructed "
   169	                f"({exc}); deferred to runtime"
   170	            )
   171	
   172	
   173	def _agent_refs(step: Step, needs_agent: bool) -> list[str]:
   174	    refs: list[str] = []
   175	    if step.agent:
   176	        refs.append(step.agent)
   177	    for key in ("message_agent", "reviewer", "triager", "fixer", "confirmer",
   178	                "escalation_agent"):
   179	        ref = step.get(key)
   180	        if ref:
   181	            refs.append(ref)
   182	    return refs
   183	
   184	
   185	def _step_uses_schema(step: Step, spec) -> bool:
   186	    return bool(spec.uses_schema or step.get("findings_schema") or step.get("schema"))

1	# Gauntlet run configuration (FR-2.1, plan P3).
     2	#
     3	# Agent profiles bind adapter + model + flags; pipeline steps reference them by
     4	# name (`agent: builder`). Swapping builder/reviewer roles is a YAML edit here or
     5	# a per-step override — no code change (FR-2.2). The engine builds the actual
     6	# adapter via the entry-point registry (FR-2.4) and lints flags at load (§8).
     7	#
     8	# `gauntlet init` will scaffold this file in P6; it is hand-authored for the
     9	# bootstrap so the P3 engine has profiles to resolve.
    10	
    11	base_branch: main
    12	branch_prefix: "gauntlet/"
    13	# Single run-root for plan/transcripts/manifests (BOOTSTRAP-NOTES #2 / #13).
    14	run_root: runs
    15	test_command: "uv run pytest"
    16	
    17	# On resume of a step killed mid-edit with a dirty worktree (review F-003):
    18	#   park          -> stop for a human (default; nothing re-run over partial work)
    19	#   reset_to_base -> back up the partial work to a ref, reset to base, re-run
    20	interrupted_step: park
    21	
    22	agents:
    23	  builder:
    24	    adapter: claude-code
    25	    model: claude-opus-latest
    26	    permission_mode: acceptEdits
    27	    allowed_tools: [Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob]
    28	    # claude loads the repo's PreToolUse hook only under project setting sources
    29	    # (pins.yaml); the engine-managed judge cannot gate the builder without it.
    30	    base_flags: ["--setting-sources", "project"]
    31	  reviewer:
    32	    adapter: codex
    33	    model: gpt-5.5
    34	    sandbox: read-only        # pure review steps are read-only (FR-9.6)
    35	  # Cheap-tier models verified live on this machine (P1/P4 contract runs; only
    36	  # an OpenAI key is present and `claude-haiku-latest` does not resolve via
    37	  # LiteLLM — see pins.yaml). FR-3.1 tiering: mini for classification, full
    38	  # model for severity-aware triage escalation (P4, review F-009).
    39	  triage:
    40	    adapter: api
    41	    model: gpt-5-mini
    42	  escalation:
    43	    adapter: api
    44	    model: gpt-5
    45	  judge_llm:
    46	    adapter: api
    47	    model: gpt-5-mini
    48	
    49	# Per-agent commit identities (FR-9.7) so git log distinguishes who authored what.
    50	identities:
    51	  builder:
    52	    name: "Gauntlet Builder (claude)"
    53	    email: "builder@gauntlet.local"
    54	  reviewer:
    55	    name: "Gauntlet Reviewer (codex)"
    56	    email: "reviewer@gauntlet.local"
    57	  triage:
    58	    name: "Gauntlet Triage"
    59	    email: "triage@gauntlet.local"

1	"""CodexAdapter: drives `codex exec` non-interactively (PRD §4.1).
     2	
     3	Verified against codex-cli 0.139.0 (see the doctor pin file): `--json` JSONL
     4	events, `--output-schema <file>`, `-o/--output-last-message`, `-s/--sandbox
     5	read-only|workspace-write`, prompt via stdin with `-`, and `codex exec resume
     6	<session>`. Note: 0.139.0 has no `--full-auto` on `exec` (the PRD/plan mention
     7	it); exec mode is already non-interactive and the sandbox flag governs write
     8	access — recorded in the pin file and BOOTSTRAP-NOTES.
     9	"""
    10	
    11	from __future__ import annotations
    12	
    13	import json
    14	import tempfile
    15	from pathlib import Path
    16	from typing import Any
    17	
    18	from gauntlet.adapters._structured import validate_schema
    19	from gauntlet.adapters.base import (
    20	    AdapterCapabilities,
    21	    AgentFailedError,
    22	    AgentResult,
    23	    AgentTimeoutError,
    24	    MalformedOutputError,
    25	    Usage,
    26	)
    27	from gauntlet.adapters.process import ProcessOutput, run_with_timeout
    28	from gauntlet.config import lint_flags
    29	
    30	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 600.0
    31	
    32	
    33	class CodexAdapter:
    34	    name = "codex"
    35	    capabilities = AdapterCapabilities(
    36	        repo_write=True, structured_output="native", resume=True
    37	    )
    38	
    39	    def __init__(
    40	        self,
    41	        *,
    42	        model: str | None = None,
    43	        sandbox: str = "read-only",
    44	        skip_git_repo_check: bool = False,
    45	        executable: str = "codex",
    46	        timeout_s: float = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S,
    47	        base_flags: list[str] | None = None,
    48	    ) -> None:
    49	        self.model = model
    50	        self.sandbox = sandbox
    51	        self.skip_git_repo_check = skip_git_repo_check
    52	        self.executable = executable
    53	        self.timeout_s = timeout_s
    54	        self.base_flags = list(base_flags or [])
    55	        lint_flags(self._build_argv(session=None, schema_path=None, output_path=None))
    56	
    57	    def run(
    58	        self,
    59	        prompt: str,
    60	        *,
    61	        session: str | None = None,
    62	        schema: dict | None = None,
    63	        cwd: Path | None = None,
    64	        extra_flags: list[str] | None = None,
    65	    ) -> AgentResult:
    66	        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="gauntlet-codex-") as tmp:
    67	            schema_path: Path | None = None
    68	            if schema is not None:
    69	                schema_path = Path(tmp) / "output-schema.json"
    70	                schema_path.write_text(json.dumps(schema))
    71	            output_path = Path(tmp) / "last-message.txt"
    72	            argv = self._build_argv(
    73	                session=session, schema_path=schema_path, output_path=output_path
    74	            )
    75	            argv += extra_flags or []
    76	            argv.append("-")  # prompt on stdin
    77	            lint_flags(argv)
    78	            out = run_with_timeout(
    79	                argv, timeout_s=self.timeout_s, stdin_text=prompt, cwd=cwd
    80	            )
    81	            last_message = (
    82	                output_path.read_text() if output_path.exists() else None
    83	            )
    84	        if out.timed_out:
    85	            raise AgentTimeoutError(
    86	                f"codex killed after {self.timeout_s}s timeout",
    87	                partial=self._partial_result(out),
    88	            )
    89	        return self._parse(out, schema=schema, last_message=last_message)
    90	
    91	    # -- command construction -------------------------------------------------
    92	
    93	    def _build_argv(
    94	        self,
    95	        *,
    96	        session: str | None,
    97	        schema_path: Path | None,
    98	        output_path: Path | None,
    99	    ) -> list[str]:
   100	        argv = [self.executable, "exec"]
   101	        if session:
   102	            argv += ["resume", session]
   103	        argv.append("--json")
   104	        if self.model:
   105	            argv += ["--model", self.model]
   106	        if session:
   107	            # `exec resume` accepts no --sandbox flag (verified on 0.139.0);
   108	            # the config-override spelling keeps the sandbox pinned on resume.
   109	            argv += ["-c", f'sandbox_mode="{self.sandbox}"']
   110	        else:
   111	            argv += ["--sandbox", self.sandbox]
   112	        if self.skip_git_repo_check:
   113	            argv.append("--skip-git-repo-check")
   114	        if schema_path is not None:
   115	            argv += ["--output-schema", str(schema_path)]
   116	        if output_path is not None:
   117	            argv += ["--output-last-message", str(output_path)]
   118	        argv += self.base_flags
   119	        return argv
   120	
   121	    # -- output parsing --------------------------------------------------------
   122	
   123	    def _parse(
   124	        self, out: ProcessOutput, *, schema: dict | None, last_message: str | None
   125	    ) -> AgentResult:
   126	        events = self._decode_events(out.stdout, strict=True, out=out)
   127	        # Fail closed on reported failure, even when output parses (F-001).
   128	        failure = self._failure_marker(out, events)
   129	        if failure:
   130	            raise AgentFailedError(
   131	                f"codex reported failure: {failure}; stderr: {out.stderr[:500]}",
   132	                partial=self._partial_result(out),
   133	            )
   134	        text = last_message if last_message is not None else self._last_agent_message(events)
   135	        if text is None:
   136	            raise MalformedOutputError(
   137	                f"no agent message in codex output (exit {out.exit_code}); "
   138	                f"stderr: {out.stderr[:500]}",
   139	                partial=self._partial_result(out),
   140	            )
   141	        structured: Any | None = None
   142	        if schema is not None:
   143	            try:
   144	                structured = json.loads(text)
   145	                validate_schema(structured, schema)
   146	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc:
   147	                raise MalformedOutputError(
   148	                    f"codex --output-schema response failed to parse/validate: {exc}",
   149	                    partial=AgentResult(
   150	                        text=text,
   151	                        session_id=self._thread_id(events),
   152	                        raw_events=events,
   153	                        exit_code=out.exit_code,
   154	                    ),
   155	                ) from exc
   156	        return AgentResult(
   157	            text=text,
   158	            structured=structured,
   159	            session_id=self._thread_id(events),
   160	            usage=self._extract_usage(events),
   161	            raw_events=events,
   162	            exit_code=out.exit_code,
   163	        )
   164	
   165	    def _decode_events(
   166	        self, stdout: str, *, strict: bool, out: ProcessOutput | None = None
   167	    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
   168	        events: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
   169	        for line in stdout.splitlines():
   170	            line = line.strip()
   171	            if not line:
   172	                continue
   173	            try:
   174	                events.append(json.loads(line))
   175	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
   176	                # Fail closed (F-002): with --json, codex logs go to stderr,
   177	                # so a non-JSON stdout line means the event contract broke.
   178	                # Lenient mode is only for building checkpointable partials.
   179	                if strict:
   180	                    raise MalformedOutputError(
   181	                        f"non-JSON line in codex --json output: {line[:200]!r}",
   182	                        partial=self._partial_result(out)
   183	                        if out is not None
   184	                        else None,
   185	                    ) from exc
   186	                events.append({"type": "gauntlet.unparsed_line", "line": line})
   187	        return events
   188	
   189	    @staticmethod
   190	    def _failure_marker(
   191	        out: ProcessOutput, events: list[dict[str, Any]]
   192	    ) -> str | None:
   193	        if out.exit_code != 0:
   194	            return f"exit code {out.exit_code}"
   195	        for event in events:
   196	            if event.get("type") in ("turn.failed", "error"):
   197	                detail = event.get("error") or event.get("message") or event
   198	                return f"{event['type']} event: {str(detail)[:300]}"
   199	        return None
   200	
   201	    @staticmethod
   202	    def _thread_id(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str | None:
   203	        for event in events:
   204	            if event.get("type") == "thread.started" and event.get("thread_id"):
   205	                return event["thread_id"]
   206	        return None
   207	
   208	    @staticmethod
   209	    def _last_agent_message(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str | None:
   210	        for event in reversed(events):
   211	            if event.get("type") == "item.completed":
   212	                item = event.get("item") or {}
   213	                if item.get("type") == "agent_message" and item.get("text"):
   214	                    return item["text"]
   215	        return None
   216	
   217	    @staticmethod
   218	    def _extract_usage(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Usage | None:
   219	        totals: dict[str, int] = {}
   220	        for event in events:
   221	            if event.get("type") == "turn.completed" and event.get("usage"):
   222	                for key, value in event["usage"].items():
   223	                    if isinstance(value, int):
   224	                        totals[key] = totals.get(key, 0) + value
   225	        if not totals:
   226	            return None
   227	        # codex reports tokens but no cost: degraded path per PRD §12 Q3.
   228	        return Usage(
   229	            input_tokens=totals.get("input_tokens"),
   230	            output_tokens=totals.get("output_tokens"),
   231	            cached_input_tokens=totals.get("cached_input_tokens"),
   232	            cost_usd=None,
   233	        )
   234	
   235	    def _partial_result(self, out: ProcessOutput) -> AgentResult:
   236	        events = self._decode_events(out.stdout, strict=False)
   237	        return AgentResult(
   238	            text=self._last_agent_message(events) or "",
   239	            session_id=self._thread_id(events),
   240	            usage=self._extract_usage(events),
   241	            raw_events=events
   242	            + [{"type": "gauntlet.stderr", "stderr": out.stderr}],
   243	            exit_code=out.exit_code if out.exit_code is not None else -1,
   244	        )

1	# Bootstrap pipeline — switchover #2 (plan P4, review F-006).
     2	#
     3	# Expresses the remaining bootstrap phases P5–P7 as a Gauntlet pipeline:
     4	# implement → tests → commit → adversarial_cycle → human gate, per phase.
     5	# From P5 onward these phases execute THROUGH `gauntlet run gauntlet-bootstrap
     6	# --pipeline pipelines/bootstrap.yaml`, human at the gates; manual process
     7	# execution past this point is a bug (plan §0 dogfooding switchover).
     8	#
     9	# This is deliberately the *minimal* expression of the loop. The polished
    10	# `standard.yaml` (prd-cycle → plan-author → foreach phases → retro) and the
    11	# full prompt set are P5 deliverables — this file is just enough machinery to
    12	# make the switchover real. Stages are written out explicitly rather than via
    13	# foreach: three phases, three stages — determinism over cleverness (§2).
    14	
    15	name: bootstrap
    16	version: 1
    17	
    18	stages:
    19	  - id: p5
    20	    steps:
    21	      - {id: p5-implement, type: agent_task, agent: builder,
    22	         prompt: prompts/bootstrap-implement-p5.md, inputs: [prd.md]}
    23	      - {id: p5-tests, type: shell, run: "{{config.test_command}}",
    24	         timeout_s: 1800, on_fail: {route_to: p5-implement, max_retries: 2}}
    25	      - {id: p5-commit, type: commit, message_agent: triage, phase: P5,
    26	         prompt: prompts/commit-message.md}
    27	      - {id: p5-cycle, type: adversarial_cycle, mode: code_review,
    28	         reviewer: reviewer, triager: triage, fixer: builder,
    29	         escalation_agent: escalation, max_rounds: 2}
    30	      - {id: p5-gate, type: human_gate,
    31	         show: [findings.json, triage.json, confirm.json]}
    32	
    33	  - id: p6
    34	    steps:
    35	      - {id: p6-implement, type: agent_task, agent: builder,
    36	         prompt: prompts/bootstrap-implement-p6.md, inputs: [prd.md]}
    37	      - {id: p6-tests, type: shell, run: "{{config.test_command}}",
    38	         timeout_s: 1800, on_fail: {route_to: p6-implement, max_retries: 2}}
    39	      - {id: p6-commit, type: commit, message_agent: triage, phase: P6,
    40	         prompt: prompts/commit-message.md}
    41	      - {id: p6-cycle, type: adversarial_cycle, mode: code_review,
    42	         reviewer: reviewer, triager: triage, fixer: builder,
    43	         escalation_agent: escalation, max_rounds: 2}
    44	      - {id: p6-gate, type: human_gate,
    45	         show: [findings.json, triage.json, confirm.json]}
    46	
    47	  - id: p7
    48	    steps:
    49	      - {id: p7-implement, type: agent_task, agent: builder,
    50	         prompt: prompts/bootstrap-implement-p7.md, inputs: [prd.md]}
    51	      - {id: p7-tests, type: shell, run: "{{config.test_command}}",
    52	         timeout_s: 1800, on_fail: {route_to: p7-implement, max_retries: 2}}
    53	      - {id: p7-commit, type: commit, message_agent: triage, phase: P7,
    54	         prompt: prompts/commit-message.md}
    55	      - {id: p7-cycle, type: adversarial_cycle, mode: code_review,
    56	         reviewer: reviewer, triager: triage, fixer: builder,
    57	         escalation_agent: escalation, max_rounds: 2}
    58	      - {id: p7-gate, type: human_gate,
    59	         show: [findings.json, triage.json, confirm.json]}

1	"""adversarial_cycle (plan P4 test strategy): full loop on scripted fakes.
     2	
     3	Covers: converge in 1 round, converge in 2, escalation on max_rounds,
     4	reviewer mutation under each FR-9.6 policy, fix-commit body content (declined
     5	findings with reasons), confirm-diff scoping (FR-9.5), schema-violation retry,
     6	prompt-injection containment (§8), severity-aware escalation (review F-009).
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import json
    12	import shutil
    13	import subprocess
    14	from pathlib import Path
    15	
    16	import pytest
    17	import yaml
    18	
    19	from gauntlet.adapters.base import AgentResult, MalformedOutputError, Usage
    20	from gauntlet.engine import gitops, manifest as M
    21	from gauntlet.engine.config import RunConfig
    22	from gauntlet.engine.cycle import DATA_BEGIN, DATA_END, needs_escalation
    23	from gauntlet.engine.manifest import Manifest, PipelineRef
    24	from gauntlet.engine.orchestrator import Orchestrator
    25	from gauntlet.engine.pipeline import Pipeline
    26	
    27	from conftest import FakeAdapter
    28	
    29	REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
    30	
    31	
    32	# --- scripted fakes -------------------------------------------------------------
    33	class SeqAdapter:
    34	    """Returns scripted responses in order; callables get (cwd) for side effects."""
    35	
    36	    capabilities = FakeAdapter.capabilities
    37	
    38	    def __init__(self, *responses):
    39	        self.responses = list(responses)
    40	        self.calls: list[dict] = []
    41	        self.timeout_s = 600.0
    42	
    43	    def run(self, prompt, *, session=None, schema=None, cwd=None, extra_flags=None):
    44	        self.calls.append({"prompt": prompt, "schema": schema})
    45	        if not self.responses:
    46	            raise AssertionError("SeqAdapter exhausted; unexpected extra call")
    47	        r = self.responses.pop(0)
    48	        if isinstance(r, Exception):
    49	            raise r
    50	        if callable(r):
    51	            r = r(cwd)
    52	        return AgentResult(
    53	            text=json.dumps(r), structured=r,
    54	            usage=Usage(input_tokens=10, output_tokens=5), exit_code=0,
    55	        )
    56	
    57	
    58	def F(fid, severity="major", claim=None):
    59	    return {
    60	        "id": fid, "severity": severity, "category": "correctness",
    61	        "location": "src.py:1", "claim": claim or f"defect {fid}",
    62	        "evidence": "seen in code", "suggested_fix": None,
    63	    }
    64	
    65	
    66	def REVIEW(*findings, summary="reviewed"):
    67	    return {"findings": list(findings), "open_questions": [], "summary": summary}
    68	
    69	
    70	def V(fid, verdict="legitimate", action="fix_now", confidence="high", **kw):
    71	    return {"finding_id": fid, "verdict": verdict, "reasoning": "1-3 sentences.",
    72	            "action": action, "confidence": confidence,
    73	            "target_artifact": None, **kw}
    74	
    75	
    76	def CV(fid, verdict="resolved"):
    77	    return {"finding_id": fid, "verdict": verdict, "notes": "checked the diff"}
    78	
    79	
    80	def CONFIRM(*verdicts, new=()):
    81	    return {"verdicts": list(verdicts), "new_findings": list(new), "summary": ""}
    82	
    83	
    84	def writer(rel, content, result):
    85	    """A SeqAdapter callable: write a file, then return ``result``."""
    86	    def _run(cwd):
    87	        target = Path(cwd) / rel
    88	        target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    89	        target.write_text(content)
    90	        return result
    91	    return _run
    92	
    93	
    94	# --- harness ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    95	BASE_CONFIG = {
    96	    "agents": {
    97	        "reviewer": {"adapter": "codex"},
    98	        "triage": {"adapter": "api", "model": "h"},
    99	        "builder": {"adapter": "claude-code"},
   100	        "esc": {"adapter": "api", "model": "strong"},
   101	    },
   102	    "identities": {
   103	        "reviewer": {"name": "Gauntlet Reviewer (codex)", "email": "reviewer@gauntlet.local"},
   104	        "builder": {"name": "Gauntlet Builder (claude)", "email": "builder@gauntlet.local"},
   105	    },
   106	}

   109	@pytest.fixture
   110	def cycle_repo(fixture_repo):
   111	    """Fixture repo with the real normative schemas + a seed artifact."""
   112	    shutil.copytree(REPO / "schemas", fixture_repo / "schemas")
   113	    (fixture_repo / "prd.md").write_text("ARTIFACT-BODY-SENTINEL\n")
   114	    subprocess.run(["git", "-C", str(fixture_repo), "add", "-A"], check=True)
   115	    subprocess.run(
   116	        ["git", "-C", str(fixture_repo), "commit", "-qm", "seed"], check=True
   117	    )
   118	    return fixture_repo

   120	
   121	def cycle_step(**extra):
   122	    step = {
   123	        "id": "cycle", "type": "adversarial_cycle", "mode": "artifact",
   124	        "artifact": "prd.md", "phase": "P5", "reviewer": "reviewer",
   125	        "triager": "triage", "fixer": "builder", "max_rounds": 2,
   126	    }
   127	    step.update(extra)
   128	    return step
   129	
   130	
   131	def run_cycle(repo, adapters, *, step_extra=None, config=None):
   132	    pipeline = Pipeline.model_validate({
   133	        "name": "demo", "version": 1,
   134	        "stages": [{"id": "s", "steps": [cycle_step(**(step_extra or {}))]}],
   135	    })
   136	    cfg = RunConfig.model_validate(config or BASE_CONFIG)
   137	    artifact_root = repo  # prd.md lives at the repo root in these tests
   138	    run_dir = repo / "runs" / "demo" / "run-1"
   139	    man = Manifest(run_id="r", slug="demo", branch="b", base_branch="main",
   140	                   pipeline=PipelineRef(name="demo", version=1, hash="h"))
   141	    orch = Orchestrator(
   142	        repo_root=repo, run_dir=run_dir, artifact_root=artifact_root,
   143	        config=cfg, pipeline=pipeline, manifest=man,
   144	        adapter_factory=lambda n: adapters[n],
   145	    )
   146	    status = orch.drive()
   147	    return status, man, run_dir

   150	# --- convergence -------------------------------------------------------------------
   151	def test_converges_in_one_round(cycle_repo):
   152	    reviewer = SeqAdapter(REVIEW(F("F-001")), CONFIRM(CV("F-001")))
   153	    adapters = {
   154	        "reviewer": reviewer,
   155	        "triage": SeqAdapter(V("F-001")),
   156	        "builder": SeqAdapter(writer("src.py", "fixed\n", {"done": True})),
   157	    }
   158	    status, man, run_dir = run_cycle(cycle_repo, adapters)
   159	    assert status == M.RUN_DONE
   160	    rec = man.record("cycle")
   161	    assert rec.status == M.DONE and "converged in round 1" in rec.notes
   162	    # one fix-round commit, fixer-attributed, enforced format (FR-9.4/9.7)
   163	    assert [c.phase for c in man.commits] == ["P5.1"]
   164	    msg = gitops.commit_message(cycle_repo, man.commits[0].sha)
   165	    assert msg.startswith("P5.1: Address review — 1 fixed, 0 declined")
   166	    author = subprocess.run(
   167	        ["git", "-C", str(cycle_repo), "log", "-1", "--format=%an <%ae>"],
   168	        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
   169	    ).stdout.strip()
   170	    assert author == "Gauntlet Builder (claude) <builder@gauntlet.local>"
   171	    # clean tree at the end: round bookkeeping never dirties the worktree
   172	    assert gitops.is_clean(cycle_repo, exclude=["runs"])
   173	    # usage from every sub-call accumulated (4 calls x 10/5)
   174	    assert rec.usage.input_tokens == 40 and rec.usage.output_tokens == 20

1	"""Run lifecycle: new / run / status / approve / reject / resume / abort / rollback.
     2	
     3	Glue between the CLI and the :class:`Orchestrator`. Owns the on-disk layout
     4	(FR-4.1), the entry contract (FR-10.1), branch management (FR-9.1), the
     5	engine-managed judge lifecycle (FR-7.1), and guarded rollback (FR-9.9 /
     6	review F-010).
     7	"""
     8	
     9	from __future__ import annotations
    10	
    11	import shutil
    12	from dataclasses import dataclass
    13	from datetime import datetime, timezone
    14	from pathlib import Path
    15	
    16	from gauntlet.engine import gitops, manifest as M
    17	from gauntlet.engine.config import RunConfig
    18	from gauntlet.engine.execution import run_bookkeeping_excludes
    19	from gauntlet.engine.judgeproc import ManagedJudge
    20	from gauntlet.engine.manifest import Manifest, PipelineRef
    21	from gauntlet.engine.orchestrator import Orchestrator
    22	from gauntlet.engine.pipeline import load_pipeline
    23	from gauntlet.engine.validate import validate_pipeline
    24	from gauntlet.logging.redact import RedactingWriter, build_redactor
    25	
    26	# Marker written into a scaffolded PRD; the entry contract refuses to run while
    27	# it is still present (FR-10.1 / review OQ-1: existence + non-stub-ness).
    28	PRD_STUB_MARKER = "<!-- GAUNTLET-PRD-STUB: replace this file with a real PRD -->"
    29	
    30	_PRD_STUB = f"""{PRD_STUB_MARKER}
    31	# PRD: <title>
    32	
    33	> Gauntlet does not author PRDs (FR-10.1). Replace this stub with a real,
    34	> human-authored PRD, then run `gauntlet run <slug>`. The run refuses to start
    35	> while this marker is present.
    36	
    37	## Problem statement
    38	
    39	## Requirements
    40	"""
    41	
    42	
    43	class EntryContractError(RuntimeError):
    44	    """The entry contract (FR-10.1) is not satisfied."""
    45	
    46	
    47	class RollbackGuardError(RuntimeError):
    48	    """A rollback guard (review F-010) refused the operation."""
    49	
    50	
    51	def _utc_stamp() -> str:
    52	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%S")
    53	
    54	
    55	def _strip_marker(text: str) -> str:
    56	    return "\n".join(line for line in text.splitlines() if PRD_STUB_MARKER not in line)
    57	
    58	
    59	def _normalize(text: str) -> str:
    60	    return "\n".join(line.strip() for line in text.strip().splitlines() if line.strip())
    61	
    62	
    63	@dataclass
    64	class RunLayout:
    65	    repo_root: Path
    66	    config: RunConfig
    67	    slug: str
    68	
    69	    @property
    70	    def slug_dir(self) -> Path:
    71	        return self.repo_root / self.config.run_root / self.slug
    72	
    73	    @property
    74	    def prd_path(self) -> Path:
    75	        return self.slug_dir / "prd.md"
    76	
    77	    @property
    78	    def active_pointer(self) -> Path:
    79	        return self.slug_dir / "active-run.txt"
    80	
    81	    def run_dir(self, name: str) -> Path:
    82	        return self.slug_dir / name
    83	
    84	    def active_run_dir(self) -> Path:
    85	        if not self.active_pointer.exists():
    86	            raise FileNotFoundError(
    87	                f"no active run for {self.slug!r}; has `gauntlet run` been started?"
    88	            )
    89	        return self.slug_dir / self.active_pointer.read_text().strip()
    90	
    91	
    92	class RunManager:
    93	    def __init__(self, repo_root: Path, config: RunConfig | None = None) -> None:
    94	        self.repo_root = repo_root
    95	        self.config = config or RunConfig.load(repo_root / ".gauntlet/config.yaml")
    96	        # The configured redaction list (FR-4.4) governs every byte the run
    97	        # writes; default-on even with an empty `redaction:` section.
    98	        self.writer = RedactingWriter(build_redactor(self.config.redaction))
    99	
   100	    def layout(self, slug: str) -> RunLayout:
   101	        return RunLayout(self.repo_root, self.config, slug)
   102	
   103	    # ---- new (FR-8.1 scaffold) ----------------------------------------------
   104	    def new(self, slug: str) -> Path:
   105	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   106	        layout.slug_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   107	        if not layout.prd_path.exists():
   108	            layout.prd_path.write_text(_PRD_STUB)
   109	        return layout.prd_path
   110	
   111	    # ---- entry contract (FR-10.1) -------------------------------------------
   112	    def check_entry_contract(self, slug: str) -> None:
   113	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   114	        if not layout.prd_path.exists():
   115	            raise EntryContractError(
   116	                f"{layout.prd_path} does not exist; `gauntlet new {slug}` scaffolds "
   117	                "a stub for a human to author (FR-10.1)"
   118	            )
   119	        content = layout.prd_path.read_text()
   120	        if PRD_STUB_MARKER in content:
   121	            raise EntryContractError(
   122	                f"{layout.prd_path} is still the scaffolded stub; a human must "
   123	                "author the PRD before a run can start (FR-10.1)"
   124	            )
   125	        # Deleting only the marker line leaves the rest of the scaffold intact —
   126	        # still not a human-authored PRD. Compare the whole body, marker-stripped
   127	        # and whitespace-normalized, against the stub (review F-007).
   128	        if _normalize(content) == _normalize(_strip_marker(_PRD_STUB)):
   129	            raise EntryContractError(
   130	                f"{layout.prd_path} is the scaffolded stub with only the marker "
   131	                "removed; a human must author a real PRD before a run (FR-10.1)"
   132	            )
   133	
   134	    # ---- run (FR-8.1) -------------------------------------------------------
   135	    def start(
   136	        self,
   137	        slug: str,
   138	        pipeline_path: Path,
   139	        *,
   140	        use_judge: bool = True,
   141	        adapter_factory=None,
   142	        extra_context: dict | None = None,
   143	        clock=None,
   144	    ) -> str:
   145	        self.check_entry_contract(slug)
   146	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   147	        pipeline, phash = load_pipeline(pipeline_path)
   148	        validate_pipeline(pipeline, self.config)
   149	
   150	        branch = f"{self.config.branch_prefix}{slug}"
   151	        gitops.checkout_or_create_branch(self.repo_root, branch, self.config.base_branch)
   152	
   153	        run_id = f"run-{_utc_stamp()}"
   154	        run_dir = layout.run_dir(run_id)
   155	        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   156	        # Snapshot the exact pipeline source into the run dir so resume reloads
   157	        # precisely what started the run (FR-5.6 reproducibility).
   158	        (run_dir / "pipeline.yaml").write_text(pipeline_path.read_text())
   159	        layout.active_pointer.write_text(run_id)
   160	
   161	        man = Manifest(
   162	            run_id=run_id,
   163	            slug=slug,
   164	            branch=branch,
   165	            base_branch=self.config.base_branch,
   166	            pipeline=PipelineRef(name=pipeline.name, version=pipeline.version, hash=phash),
   167	            prompt_hashes=self._prompt_hashes(pipeline),
   168	        )
   169	        return self._drive(
   170	            layout, run_dir, pipeline, man,
   171	            use_judge=use_judge, adapter_factory=adapter_factory,
   172	            extra_context=extra_context, clock=clock,
   173	        )
   174	
   175	    # ---- resume (FR-8.2) ----------------------------------------------------
   176	    def resume(self, slug: str, *, use_judge: bool = True, adapter_factory=None,
   177	               extra_context: dict | None = None, clock=None) -> str:
   178	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   179	        run_dir = layout.active_run_dir()
   180	        man = Manifest.load(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   181	        pipeline, phash = load_pipeline(run_dir / "pipeline.yaml")
   182	        if phash != man.pipeline.hash:
   183	            raise RuntimeError(
   184	                "pipeline content hash changed since the run started "
   185	                f"({man.pipeline.hash} -> {phash}); resume refuses to run a "
   186	                "different pipeline against an existing manifest (FR-5.6)"
   187	            )
   188	        gitops.checkout_or_create_branch(self.repo_root, man.branch, man.base_branch)
   189	        return self._drive(
   190	            layout, run_dir, pipeline, man,
   191	            use_judge=use_judge, adapter_factory=adapter_factory,
   192	            extra_context=extra_context, clock=clock,
   193	        )
   194	
   195	    # ---- gates --------------------------------------------------------------
   196	    def approve(self, slug: str, gate: str | None = None, notes: str | None = None,
   197	                *, use_judge: bool = True, adapter_factory=None) -> str:
   198	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   199	        run_dir = layout.active_run_dir()
   200	        man = Manifest.load(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   201	        gate = gate or man.current_step
   202	        if gate is None:
   203	            raise ValueError("no gate to approve; run is not parked")
   204	        pipeline, _ = load_pipeline(run_dir / "pipeline.yaml")
   205	        # Approving a gate drives the rest of the run, so honor use_judge for it.
   206	        if use_judge:
   207	            return self._with_judge(man, run_dir, lambda env: self._approve_drive(
   208	                layout, run_dir, pipeline, man, gate, notes, env, adapter_factory))
   209	        orch = self._orchestrator(layout, run_dir, pipeline, man,
   210	                                  judge_env={}, adapter_factory=adapter_factory)
   211	        return orch.approve_gate(gate, notes)
   212	
   213	    def reject(self, slug: str, notes: str, gate: str | None = None) -> str:
   214	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   215	        run_dir = layout.active_run_dir()
   216	        man = Manifest.load(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   217	        gate = gate or man.current_step
   218	        if gate is None:
   219	            raise ValueError("no gate to reject; run is not parked")
   220	        pipeline, _ = load_pipeline(run_dir / "pipeline.yaml")
   221	        orch = self._orchestrator(layout, run_dir, pipeline, man, judge_env={})
   222	        return orch.reject_gate(gate, notes)
   223	
   224	    # ---- abort --------------------------------------------------------------
   225	    def abort(self, slug: str) -> str:
   226	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   227	        run_dir = layout.active_run_dir()
   228	        man = Manifest.load(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   229	        man.status = M.RUN_ABORTED
   230	        man.write_atomic(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   231	        return man.status
   232	
   233	    # ---- status -------------------------------------------------------------
   234	    def status(self, slug: str) -> Manifest:
   235	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   236	        return Manifest.load(layout.active_run_dir() / "manifest.json")
   237	
   238	    # ---- rollback (FR-9.9 / review F-010) -----------------------------------
   239	    def rollback(self, slug: str, phase: int) -> str:
   240	        layout = self.layout(slug)
   241	        run_dir = layout.active_run_dir()
   242	        man = Manifest.load(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   243	
   244	        # Guard 1: clean work tree — only the engine's own bookkeeping is
   245	        # excluded (review F-001), so an uncommitted real artifact still blocks.
   246	        excludes = run_bookkeeping_excludes(self.repo_root, run_dir, layout.slug_dir)
   247	        if not gitops.is_clean(self.repo_root, exclude=excludes):
   248	            raise RollbackGuardError(
   249	                "refusing rollback: worktree is dirty; commit or discard first"
   250	            )
   251	        # Guard 2: branch tip MUST equal the manifest's last recorded commit.
   252	        # A branch ahead of the manifest (extra unmanifested commits) is a
   253	        # divergence — reset would silently discard those commits (review F-003).
   254	        if not man.commits:
   255	            raise RollbackGuardError("no recorded commits to roll back to")
   256	        last_recorded = man.commits[-1].sha
   257	        head = gitops.head_sha(self.repo_root)
   258	        if head != last_recorded:
   259	            raise RollbackGuardError(
   260	                "refusing rollback: branch has diverged from the manifest "
   261	                f"(HEAD {head[:10]} != last recorded {last_recorded[:10]}); the "
   262	                "branch and manifest must agree before a rewind (FR-9.9)"
   263	            )
   264	        # Resolve the target: the last commit whose phase prefix is P<phase>.
   265	        target = self._phase_boundary_sha(man, phase)
   266	        if target is None:
   267	            raise RollbackGuardError(
   268	                f"no recorded phase-{phase} commit boundary to roll back to"
   269	            )
   270	
   271	        # Backup ref + manifest snapshot before any rewind (F-010).
   272	        ts = _utc_stamp()
   273	        gitops.create_ref(
   274	            self.repo_root, f"refs/gauntlet/backup/{man.run_id}/{ts}", head
   275	        )
   276	        shutil.copy2(run_dir / "manifest.json", run_dir / f"manifest.snapshot-{ts}.json")
   277	
   278	        gitops.reset_hard(self.repo_root, target)
   279	        self._rewind_manifest(man, run_dir, target)
   280	        man.write_atomic(run_dir / "manifest.json")
   281	        return target
   282	
   283	    def _rewind_manifest(self, man: Manifest, run_dir: Path, target: str) -> None:
   284	        """Rewind the manifest to match the reset branch (review F-002).
   285	
   286	        Drop commits after the target, and reset to `pending` EVERY step record
   287	        (any type, any iteration) that executes after the target phase boundary
   288	        in pipeline order — not just the steps that produced dropped commits.
   289	        Otherwise a later resume skips work `git reset --hard` removed and the
   290	        branch and manifest disagree (FR-9.9).
   291	        """
   292	        keep: list = []
   293	        for commit in man.commits:
   294	            keep.append(commit)
   295	            if commit.sha == target:
   296	                break
   297	        man.commits = keep
   298	        target_step = keep[-1].step_id
   299	
   300	        pipeline, _ = load_pipeline(run_dir / "pipeline.yaml")

1	"""Triage-accuracy evaluation (plan P4 assumption test, review F-009).
     2	
     3	The P4 assumption is "cheap-model triage is accurate enough": ≥ 85% verdict
     4	agreement with the hand-labeled bootstrap corpus AND zero blocking-severity
     5	findings misclassified into a reject category *without escalation*, reported
     6	as a per-severity confusion matrix — aggregate agreement alone can hide a
     7	catastrophic blocking false-negative (review F-009).
     8	
     9	The math lives here (unit-tested, offline); the live measurement is the
    10	integration test, which runs the configured cheap model over the corpus with
    11	the exact prompt the cycle ships (:func:`gauntlet.engine.cycle.triage_prompt`).
    12	"""
    13	
    14	from __future__ import annotations
    15	
    16	import json
    17	from collections import Counter
    18	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    19	from pathlib import Path
    20	from typing import Any
    21	
    22	from gauntlet.engine.cycle import REJECT_VERDICTS, needs_escalation
    23	
    24	VERDICTS = ("legitimate", "bikeshedding", "premature_optimization", "not_applicable")
    25	SEVERITIES = ("blocking", "major", "minor", "nit")
    26	
    27	
    28	@dataclass
    29	class CorpusEntry:
    30	    id: str
    31	    source: str
    32	    context: str
    33	    finding: dict[str, Any]
    34	    label: dict[str, str]
    35	
    36	    @property
    37	    def severity(self) -> str:
    38	        return self.finding.get("severity", "")
    39	
    40	
    41	def load_corpus(path: Path) -> list[CorpusEntry]:
    42	    entries = []
    43	    for line in path.read_text().splitlines():
    44	        if not line.strip():
    45	            continue
    46	        raw = json.loads(line)
    47	        entries.append(CorpusEntry(**raw))
    48	    return entries
    49	
    50	
    51	@dataclass
    52	class EvalReport:
    53	    total: int = 0
    54	    agreements: int = 0
    55	    # per-severity confusion: {severity: Counter[(label_verdict, predicted_verdict)]}
    56	    confusion: dict[str, Counter] = field(default_factory=dict)
    57	    # blocking findings whose predicted verdict landed in a reject category
    58	    # while the label did not — split by whether escalation caught them.
    59	    blocking_misses_escalated: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    60	    blocking_misses_unescalated: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    61	    action_agreements: int = 0
    62	    disagreements: list[dict[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)
    63	
    64	    @property
    65	    def agreement(self) -> float:
    66	        return self.agreements / self.total if self.total else 0.0
    67	
    68	    @property
    69	    def action_agreement(self) -> float:
    70	        return self.action_agreements / self.total if self.total else 0.0
    71	
    72	    def passes_exit_criteria(self) -> bool:
    73	        """Plan P4 exit: ≥85% agreement AND zero unescalated blocking misses."""
    74	        return self.agreement >= 0.85 and not self.blocking_misses_unescalated
    75	
    76	
    77	def evaluate(
    78	    entries: list[CorpusEntry], predictions: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]
    79	) -> EvalReport:
    80	    """Score model verdicts against the hand labels.
    81	
    82	    ``predictions`` maps corpus-entry id → the model's verdict object
    83	    (``verdict``/``action``/``confidence``). Escalation is judged with the
    84	    shipped :func:`needs_escalation` rule, so the guarantee measured is the
    85	    guarantee deployed.
    86	    """
    87	    report = EvalReport()
    88	    for entry in entries:
    89	        pred = predictions[entry.id]
    90	        report.total += 1
    91	        label_v = entry.label["verdict"]
    92	        pred_v = pred.get("verdict", "")
    93	        severity = entry.severity
    94	        report.confusion.setdefault(severity, Counter())[(label_v, pred_v)] += 1
    95	        if pred_v == label_v:
    96	            report.agreements += 1
    97	        else:
    98	            report.disagreements.append({
    99	                "id": entry.id, "severity": severity,
   100	                "label": label_v, "predicted": pred_v,
   101	                "confidence": pred.get("confidence", "?"),
   102	                "reasoning": pred.get("reasoning", ""),
   103	            })
   104	        if pred.get("action") == entry.label.get("action"):
   105	            report.action_agreements += 1
   106	        if (
   107	            severity == "blocking"
   108	            and pred_v in REJECT_VERDICTS
   109	            and label_v not in REJECT_VERDICTS
   110	        ):
   111	            if needs_escalation(severity, pred):
   112	                report.blocking_misses_escalated.append(entry.id)
   113	            else:  # structurally unreachable while the rule escalates all
   114	                report.blocking_misses_unescalated.append(entry.id)  # blockers
   115	    return report
   116	
   117	
   118	def render_report(report: EvalReport, *, model: str, corpus_path: str) -> str:
   119	    """Markdown report: the recorded artifact the P4 exit criteria ask for."""
   120	    lines = [
   121	        "# Triage accuracy — P4 assumption test (review F-009)",
   122	        "",
   123	        f"- model: `{model}`",
   124	        f"- corpus: `{corpus_path}` ({report.total} hand-labeled findings)",
   125	        f"- verdict agreement: **{report.agreement:.1%}** "
   126	        f"({report.agreements}/{report.total}; exit ≥ 85%)",
   127	        f"- action agreement (secondary): {report.action_agreement:.1%}",
   128	        f"- blocking→reject misses without escalation: "
   129	        f"**{len(report.blocking_misses_unescalated)}** (exit: zero)"
   130	        + (f" — {report.blocking_misses_unescalated}"
   131	           if report.blocking_misses_unescalated else ""),
   132	        f"- blocking→reject misses caught by escalation: "
   133	        f"{len(report.blocking_misses_escalated)}"
   134	        + (f" — {report.blocking_misses_escalated}"
   135	           if report.blocking_misses_escalated else ""),
   136	        f"- exit criteria: {'**PASS**' if report.passes_exit_criteria() else '**FAIL**'}",
   137	        "",
   138	        "## Per-severity confusion matrices (label rows × predicted columns)",
   139	    ]
   140	    for severity in SEVERITIES:
   141	        counter = report.confusion.get(severity)
   142	        if not counter:
   143	            continue
   144	        n = sum(counter.values())
   145	        lines += ["", f"### {severity} (n={n})", ""]
   146	        lines.append("| label \\ predicted | " + " | ".join(VERDICTS) + " |")
   147	        lines.append("|---|" + "---|" * len(VERDICTS))
   148	        labels = sorted({lab for (lab, _p) in counter})
   149	        for lab in labels:
   150	            row = [str(counter.get((lab, p), 0)) for p in VERDICTS]
   151	            lines.append(f"| {lab} | " + " | ".join(row) + " |")
   152	    if report.disagreements:
   153	        lines += ["", "## Disagreements", ""]
   154	        for d in report.disagreements:
   155	            lines.append(
   156	                f"- `{d['id']}` ({d['severity']}): labeled **{d['label']}**, "
   157	                f"model said **{d['predicted']}** "
   158	                f"(confidence {d['confidence']}) — {d['reasoning']}"
   159	            )
   160	    lines += [
   161	        "",
   162	        "## Corpus caveat (recorded honestly)",
   163	        "",
   164	        "The corpus is harvested from the bootstrap's own plan/P1/P2/P3 review "
   165	        "rounds, where almost every finding was triaged `legitimate` (34/36); "
   166	        "`nit` severity never occurred. A constant-`legitimate` predictor would "
   167	        "score ~94% — the aggregate gate is therefore weak on this data, which "
   168	        "is exactly why the blocking-miss criterion and the per-severity matrix "
   169	        "are the operative checks (review F-009). FR-6.5's human-corrected "
   170	        "cases are the designed mechanism for growing the non-legitimate side.",
   171	    ]
   172	    return "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
590	    assert (steps / "r1-confirm" / "confirm.json").exists()

1	"""Pipeline model + loader (FR-5).
     2	
     3	A pipeline is a YAML document: ``name``, ``version``, and ordered ``stages``,
     4	each with ordered ``steps``. Steps carry first-class ``when:``/``foreach:``/
     5	``on_fail:`` attributes and per-step overrides (FR-5.4). Unknown keys are
     6	preserved (``extra="allow"``) so custom step types (FR-5.5) and type-specific
     7	fields need no model change. Versioning is ``version:`` + a content hash of the
     8	exact bytes loaded (FR-5.6), both recorded in the manifest.
     9	"""
    10	
    11	from __future__ import annotations
    12	
    13	import hashlib
    14	from pathlib import Path
    15	from typing import Any
    16	
    17	import yaml
    18	from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field
    19	
    20	
    21	class OnFail(BaseModel):
    22	    """Failure routing for a step (FR-5.4)."""
    23	
    24	    route_to: str
    25	    max_retries: int = 0
    26	
    27	
    28	class Step(BaseModel):
    29	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
    30	
    31	    id: str
    32	    type: str
    33	
    34	    # first-class control attributes (FR-5.4)
    35	    agent: str | None = None
    36	    when: str | None = None
    37	    foreach: str | None = None
    38	    on_fail: OnFail | None = None
    39	
    40	    # per-step overrides (FR-5.4) — engine-level budget guards (FR-3.3)
    41	    max_turns: int | None = None
    42	    budget_usd: float | None = None
    43	    timeout_s: float | None = None
    44	
    45	    def get(self, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
    46	        """Read a type-specific field — declared or extra.
    47	
    48	        Deliberately consults only model fields and ``extra``; using ``hasattr``
    49	        would collide with pydantic's own methods (``schema``, ``dict``, ``copy``)
    50	        and return a bound method for, e.g., a step's ``schema:`` key.
    51	        """
    52	        if key in type(self).model_fields:
    53	            return getattr(self, key)
    54	        extra = self.__pydantic_extra__ or {}
    55	        return extra.get(key, default)
    56	
    57	
    58	class Stage(BaseModel):
    59	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
    60	
    61	    id: str
    62	    foreach: str | None = None
    63	    when: str | None = None
    64	    steps: list[Step]
    65	
    66	
    67	class Pipeline(BaseModel):
    68	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")
    69	
    70	    name: str
    71	    version: int
    72	    stages: list[Stage]
    73	
    74	    def all_steps(self) -> list[Step]:
    75	        return [step for stage in self.stages for step in stage.steps]
    76	
    77	
    78	def content_hash(text: str) -> str:
    79	    """Stable content hash of the pipeline source (FR-5.6)."""
    80	    return "sha256:" + hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    81	
    82	
    83	def load_pipeline(path: Path) -> tuple[Pipeline, str]:
    84	    """Load and parse a pipeline file; return ``(pipeline, content_hash)``.
    85	
    86	    Parsing only — semantic load-time validation (dangling artifacts, adapter
    87	    capabilities, banned flags) lives in :mod:`gauntlet.engine.validate` so the
    88	    model stays free of cross-module imports.
    89	    """
    90	    if not path.exists():
    91	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"pipeline not found at {path}")
    92	    text = path.read_text()
    93	    data = yaml.safe_load(text)
    94	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
    95	        raise ValueError(f"{path} must be a YAML mapping, got {type(data).__name__}")
    96	    pipeline = Pipeline.model_validate(data)
    97	    _assert_unique_ids(pipeline)
    98	    return pipeline, content_hash(text)
    99	
   100	
   101	def _assert_unique_ids(pipeline: Pipeline) -> None:
   102	    seen: set[str] = set()
   103	    for step in pipeline.all_steps():
   104	        if step.id in seen:
   105	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate step id {step.id!r} in pipeline")
   106	        seen.add(step.id)
   107	    stage_ids: set[str] = set()
   108	    for stage in pipeline.stages:
   109	        if stage.id in stage_ids:
   110	            raise ValueError(f"duplicate stage id {stage.id!r} in pipeline")
   111	        stage_ids.add(stage.id)

1	"""Run manifest: the state-machine checkpoint (§7, FR-8.2, review F-003).
     2	
     3	The manifest is written **write-ahead** — before and after every step — and
     4	atomically (write-temp + ``os.replace``), so a ``kill -9`` at any instant
     5	leaves either the prior or the next consistent state on disk, never a torn
     6	file. The side-effect transaction boundary (review F-003) lives here too: each
     7	worktree-touching step records its **base SHA** before running, so resume can
     8	tell a clean re-entry from a step that died mid-edit.
     9	"""
    10	
    11	from __future__ import annotations
    12	
    13	import json
    14	import os
    15	import tempfile
    16	from pathlib import Path
    17	from typing import Any
    18	
    19	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
    20	
    21	# --- step lifecycle states ---------------------------------------------------
    22	PENDING = "pending"
    23	RUNNING = "running"
    24	DONE = "done"
    25	FAILED = "failed"
    26	INTERRUPTED = "interrupted"  # killed mid-step, dirty worktree (F-003)
    27	PARKED = "parked"  # human_gate / interrupted-park awaiting a human
    28	HALTED = "halted"  # budget/timeout guard tripped (FR-3.3)
    29	SKIPPED = "skipped"  # `when:` false
    30	
    31	# --- run lifecycle states ----------------------------------------------------
    32	RUN_RUNNING = "running"
    33	RUN_PARKED = "parked"
    34	RUN_DONE = "done"
    35	RUN_ABORTED = "aborted"
    36	RUN_FAILED = "failed"
    37	
    38	
    39	class PipelineRef(BaseModel):
    40	    name: str
    41	    version: int
    42	    hash: str
    43	
    44	
    45	class UsageTotals(BaseModel):
    46	    input_tokens: int = 0
    47	    output_tokens: int = 0
    48	    cached_input_tokens: int = 0
    49	    cost_usd: float | None = None  # None until at least one priced call (§12 Q3)
    50	
    51	    def add(self, usage: Any | None) -> None:
    52	        if usage is None:
    53	            return
    54	        self.input_tokens += usage.input_tokens or 0
    55	        self.output_tokens += usage.output_tokens or 0
    56	        self.cached_input_tokens += usage.cached_input_tokens or 0
    57	        if usage.cost_usd is not None:
    58	            self.cost_usd = (self.cost_usd or 0.0) + usage.cost_usd
    59	
    60	
    61	class StepRecord(BaseModel):
    62	    id: str
    63	    type: str
    64	    status: str = PENDING
    65	    agent: str | None = None
    66	    session_id: str | None = None
    67	    started: str | None = None
    68	    ended: str | None = None
    69	    attempts: int = 0
    70	    # Transaction boundary (F-003): HEAD before the step touched the worktree.
    71	    base_sha: str | None = None
    72	    usage: UsageTotals = Field(default_factory=UsageTotals)
    73	    notes: str | None = None
    74	    # foreach binding key, when this record is one iteration of a fan-out step
    75	    iteration: str | None = None
    76	
    77	
    78	class CommitRecord(BaseModel):
    79	    step_id: str
    80	    phase: str  # the PN[.x] prefix
    81	    sha: str
    82	
    83	
    84	class Manifest(BaseModel):
    85	    """The persisted run state (§7)."""
    86	
    87	    run_id: str
    88	    slug: str
    89	    branch: str
    90	    base_branch: str
    91	    pipeline: PipelineRef
    92	    prompt_hashes: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    93	    status: str = RUN_RUNNING
    94	    current_step: str | None = None
    95	    steps: list[StepRecord] = Field(default_factory=list)
    96	    commits: list[CommitRecord] = Field(default_factory=list)
    97	    totals: UsageTotals = Field(default_factory=UsageTotals)
    98	
    99	    # ---- record lookup -------------------------------------------------------
   100	    def record(self, step_id: str, iteration: str | None = None) -> StepRecord | None:
   101	        for rec in self.steps:
   102	            if rec.id == step_id and rec.iteration == iteration:
   103	                return rec
   104	        return None
   105	
   106	    def upsert(self, rec: StepRecord) -> StepRecord:
   107	        for i, existing in enumerate(self.steps):
   108	            if existing.id == rec.id and existing.iteration == rec.iteration:
   109	                self.steps[i] = rec
   110	                return rec
   111	        self.steps.append(rec)
   112	        return rec
   113	
   114	    # ---- atomic persistence (FR-8.2) ----------------------------------------
   115	    def write_atomic(self, path: Path) -> None:
   116	        """Write the manifest atomically: temp file in the same dir + replace.
   117	
   118	        ``os.replace`` is atomic on POSIX within a filesystem, so a reader (or a
   119	        resume after kill) always sees a whole manifest — the prior one until
   120	        the instant the new one lands. ``fsync`` before replace so the bytes are
   121	        durable, not just in the page cache, before the rename is visible.
   122	        """
   123	        path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   124	        payload = self.model_dump_json(indent=2)
   125	        fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=path.parent, prefix=".manifest-", suffix=".tmp")
   126	        try:
   127	            with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as fh:
   128	                fh.write(payload)
   129	                fh.flush()
   130	                os.fsync(fh.fileno())
   131	            os.replace(tmp, path)
   132	        except BaseException:
   133	            # On any failure (including KeyboardInterrupt) leave the prior
   134	            # manifest untouched and clean up the temp file.
   135	            try:
   136	                os.unlink(tmp)
   137	            except FileNotFoundError:
   138	                pass
   139	            raise
   140	
   141	    @classmethod
   142	    def load(cls, path: Path) -> Manifest:
   143	        return cls.model_validate_json(path.read_text())
